All Characters in Super Bear Adventure - NPCs, Enemies & Bosses
Complete guide to every character in Super Bear Adventure. Meet all NPCs, enemies, bosses, and friendly creatures across every world in Earthkwak.

## Every Character in Super Bear Adventure

The world of Super Bear Adventure is teeming with life. From friendly NPCs who offer quests and wisdom to dangerous enemies guarding every corner of the Kingdom of Earthkwak, the game's character roster is both diverse and charming. This comprehensive guide covers every character type you will encounter during your adventure.

Whether you are looking for a specific NPC to complete a quest, trying to understand an enemy's attack patterns, or simply curious about the game's cast, this guide has the information you need. For gameplay strategies involving these characters, check out our [beginner guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) and individual world walkthroughs.

## The Protagonist: Baaren

![Baaren hero character](/images/characters/baaren-hero.svg)

### Overview

Baaren is the heroic bear you control throughout Super Bear Adventure. As the player character, Baaren is defined largely by the player's choices, but several traits remain consistent across all playthroughs.

### Appearance and Customization

Baaren's default appearance is that of a friendly brown bear with expressive eyes and a determined expression. However, players can extensively customize Baaren's look through the game's [skin and hat system](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/). Available customizations include:

- **Skins**: Full body appearance changes ranging from color variants to themed outfits
- **Hats**: Headwear options from simple caps to elaborate themed accessories
- **Accessories**: Additional cosmetic items that personalize Baaren's appearance
- **Color options**: Some skins allow color customization for individual elements

### Abilities

Baaren possesses a versatile set of abilities that expand throughout the game:

- **Movement**: Running, jumping, double jumping, wall sliding
- **Combat**: Basic attacks, ground pound, charge attack
- **Traversal**: Swimming, climbing, ice sliding (world-specific)
- **Special**: Interaction with objects, puzzle solving, vehicle operation

For more about Baaren's story and background, visit our [complete lore guide](/blog/lore-baaren-story/).

## Friendly NPCs by World

![Friendly NPCs](/images/characters/friendly-npcs.svg)

### Turtletown NPCs

Turtletown is home to some of the friendliest characters in the game, making it the perfect introduction to Earthkwak's inhabitants. Full exploration details are in our [Turtletown walkthrough](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/).

#### Elder Tortuga

The oldest and wisest turtle in Turtletown, Elder Tortuga serves as your initial guide to the world. He provides:
- Tutorial instructions for new players
- Historical information about Turtletown
- Early game quests that introduce core mechanics
- Lore dialogue about the Kingdom of Earthkwak

#### Shelly the Shopkeeper

Shelly runs the main shop in Turtletown. She offers:
- Basic cosmetic items at affordable prices
- Hints about hidden items in exchange for coins
- Commentary on current events in the kingdom
- Seasonal items during holiday events

#### Captain Flipper

A retired sea turtle who can be found near the harbor. Captain Flipper:
- Tells stories about his travels across distant seas
- Offers water-based missions
- Provides information about underwater areas
- Gives tips about swimming mechanics

#### The Turtle Patrol

A group of three young turtles who patrol the outskirts of Turtletown:
- They alert Baaren to nearby threats
- Offer simple fetch quests for beginners
- Each has a distinct personality (brave, cautious, and curious)
- Their dialogue changes as you progress through the game

### Snow Valley NPCs

The frozen north is inhabited by hardy creatures who have adapted to the extreme cold. Discover all their secrets in our [Snow Valley guide](/blog/snow-valley-secrets/).

#### Professor Frost

A scholarly penguin who studies the ancient artifacts frozen in the ice:
- Provides information about Snow Valley's history
- Offers puzzle-based quests related to ancient mechanisms
- His research notes contain important lore details
- Appears in multiple locations as you unlock new areas

#### Yuki the Snow Fox

A nimble white fox who serves as a guide through Snow Valley's treacherous terrain:
- Marks safe paths through blizzard areas
- Offers speed-based challenges
- Shares survival tips about the cold environment
- Has a side quest involving finding lost fox cubs

#### The Penguin Community

An entire village of penguins inhabits the more sheltered areas of Snow Valley:
- The Village Chief assigns community-based missions
- Young penguins offer mini-game challenges
- The Penguin Cook seeks rare ingredients for recipes
- The Penguin Craftsman can create special items from materials found in the snow

#### The Hermit Bear

A reclusive bear who lives alone in a cave high in the mountains:
- Provides some of the deepest lore dialogue in the game
- Offers challenging quests with significant rewards
- His backstory connects to Baaren's own journey
- Finding him requires navigating difficult platforming sections

### Beemothep Desert NPCs

The desert is home to characters connected to the ancient civilization that once thrived there. Our [Beemothep Desert guide](/blog/beemothep-desert-puzzle/) covers the full region.

#### The Desert Oracle

A mysterious figure who appears near the ancient ruins:
- Speaks in riddles that hint at puzzle solutions
- Provides cryptic warnings about desert dangers
- Offers quests related to uncovering ancient artifacts
- Their true identity is one of the game's mysteries

#### Scarab the Merchant

A beetle NPC who runs a trading post in the desert:
- Sells desert-specific items and equipment
- Trades rare collectibles for unique rewards
- Provides information about desert landmarks
- Offers a daily rotating inventory of special items

#### The Archaeologist Lizard

A lizard character dedicated to studying the desert ruins:
- Assigns excavation-related missions
- Translates hieroglyphics that reveal lore
- Competes with the Oracle for the most cryptic dialogue
- Found in different locations as new dig sites open up

### Giant House NPCs

The Giant House has a unique set of tiny characters navigating the oversized environment. See our [Giant House walkthrough](/blog/giant-house-walkthrough/).

#### The Mouse Family

A family of mice who have made the Giant House their home:
- Father Mouse provides main quests in this world
- Mother Mouse offers healing and save point access
- Baby mice are scattered as collectibles to rescue
- They provide contextual dialogue about the house's mysteries

#### Bug Brigade

A group of organized insects who patrol the house:
- Ant soldiers serve as both allies and quest givers
- The Spider Weaver offers traversal tips using web platforms
- Ladybug the Navigator provides minimap information
- Beetle the Builder has crafting-related quests

### The Hive NPCs

The bee society in The Hive is one of the most organized communities in the game. Full details in our [Hive walkthrough](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/).

#### Queen Bee

The ruler of The Hive and a pivotal story character:
- Provides the primary quest line in The Hive
- Her dialogue reveals political tensions within bee society
- Offers substantial rewards for completing her requests
- Central to The Hive's boss encounter narrative

#### Worker Bee Representatives

Each section of The Hive has worker bee NPCs:
- Honey Storage Workers explain the economy of The Hive
- Builder Bees discuss the architecture and expansion plans
- Guard Bees patrol borders and offer combat-related quests
- Nursery Bees care for larvae and have collection quests

#### The Rebel Bee

A dissenting bee who questions The Hive's strict hierarchy:
- Offers alternative perspective quests
- Hidden in a secret area within The Hive
- His questline explores themes of individuality vs. community
- Completing his quests unlocks a unique cosmetic reward

### Mole Mines NPCs

The underground world is populated by hardworking miners with rich stories. See our [Mole Mines walkthrough](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/).

#### Foreman Digger

The head mole who manages mining operations:
- Assigns main story quests in the Mole Mines
- Provides information about mine safety and hazards
- His dialogue hints at discoveries deep underground
- Offers mine cart access to different sections

#### The Gem Collector

An eccentric mole obsessed with rare minerals:
- Collection quests for various gem types
- Pays premium prices for rare finds
- His collection room is a hidden area worth discovering
- Completing his collection unlocks a special reward

#### The Mine Ghost

A spectral figure encountered in the deepest tunnels:
- Provides lore about the mines' ancient history
- Appears and disappears mysteriously during gameplay
- His questline is one of the most atmospheric in the game
- Connects to the broader [Earthkwak lore](/blog/lore-baaren-story/)

## Enemies by World

![Enemy types across worlds](/images/characters/enemy-types.svg)

### Common Enemies Across All Worlds

Some enemy types appear throughout Earthkwak with visual variations matching each world's theme:

#### Slimes

- Found in every world with color variants
- Basic melee enemies with simple movement patterns
- Easy to defeat but can be overwhelming in groups
- Color indicates element: green (forest), blue (ice), red (fire), yellow (desert)

#### Bats

- Appear in enclosed areas across all worlds
- Fly in predictable patterns
- Attack by swooping down at the player
- Vulnerable during their dive animation

### Turtletown Enemies

#### Crabby Guards

- Aggressive crabs that patrol beach areas
- Attack with pincer snaps at close range
- Move sideways to dodge attacks
- Defeated with two standard hits or one ground pound

#### Jellyfish Drifters

- Float above water areas
- Damage on contact through electric shock
- Cannot be defeated with standard attacks
- Must be avoided or removed with ground pound from above

#### Seagull Divers

- Flying enemies that swoop down from above
- Difficult to hit during their flight pattern
- Vulnerable when they land to rest
- Drop useful items when defeated

### Snow Valley Enemies

#### Ice Golems

- Large, slow enemies made of ice
- Take multiple hits to defeat
- Their ground pound creates ice waves
- Weakness: fire-element attacks or rapid combos

#### Frost Wolves

- Fast-moving pack hunters
- Attack in coordinated groups
- Howl before charging to telegraph attacks
- Defeating the alpha wolf causes others to flee

#### Snowball Throwers

- Small creatures that attack from range
- Hide behind snow mounds for cover
- Snowballs slow Baaren temporarily
- Flank them to avoid ranged attacks

### Beemothep Desert Enemies

#### Sand Scorpions

- Burrow underground and ambush from below
- Visible by sand disturbance before attack
- Tail strike is their primary attack
- Dodging the initial attack creates a vulnerability window

#### Mummy Wraps

- Slow-moving enemies found in tombs
- Grab and hold Baaren if they get close
- Resistant to frontal attacks
- Attack from behind or above for best results

#### Sandstorm Sprites

- Small enemies that appear during sandstorms
- Difficult to see in low visibility conditions
- Deal damage through contact
- Move to sheltered areas to avoid them

### Giant House Enemies

#### Kitchen Roaches

- Fast-moving enemies that scatter when approached
- Small and hard to hit
- Attack in large numbers
- Ground pound is most effective against groups

#### Dust Bunnies

- Floating enemies that drift through rooms
- Cause sneeze effects that stun Baaren briefly
- Dissipate with a single hit
- Reform after a short time if the room is not cleared

### The Hive Enemies

#### Drone Soldiers

- Organized military bees defending The Hive
- Attack with sting charges
- Coordinate attacks with nearby drones
- Vulnerable after their charge attack misses

#### Honey Slimes

- Sticky variants of standard slimes
- Contact slows Baaren's movement
- Leave trails of honey that affect terrain
- Must be defeated quickly before they cover the area

### Mole Mines Enemies

#### Crystal Crabs

- Underground crabs with crystalline shells
- Highly resistant to damage from certain angles
- Must be attacked from behind or below
- Drop valuable crystal items when defeated

#### Cave Spiders

- Web-spinning enemies that create obstacles
- Can trap Baaren in webs
- Sensitive to light from certain items
- Defeating them clears web barriers in the area

## Boss Characters

![The Glitch secret boss](/images/characters/the-glitch.svg)

Every world features memorable boss encounters that test your skills. Our [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) covers detailed tactics for each one.

### Turtletown Boss: The Corrupted Guardian

A once-peaceful guardian of Turtletown's shores who has been turned hostile:
- Three-phase fight with increasing difficulty
- Weak point on the back exposed during specific attacks
- Environmental hazards during later phases
- Defeating it restores peace to the coastal area

### Snow Valley Boss: The Frost Warden

An ancient ice entity awakened by the disturbance in Earthkwak:
- Creates ice barriers that must be destroyed to access weak points
- Summons minion ice creatures during the fight
- The arena changes with ice and snow mechanics
- Understanding its pattern is key to victory

### Beemothep Desert Boss: The Pharaoh's Shadow

A spectral guardian of the desert's deepest tomb:
- Uses ancient magic attacks with wide area effects
- Creates mirror images that must be identified
- Sand mechanics affect movement during the fight
- One of the most lore-significant boss encounters

### Giant House Boss: The House Cat

The enormous house cat that views Baaren as a toy:
- Uses paw swipes, pounces, and roll attacks
- The arena involves climbing furniture to reach safe positions
- Must use the environment to create counterattack opportunities
- One of the most visually impressive boss fights in the game

### The Hive Boss: The Royal Guard

The queen's elite protector and a formidable bee warrior:
- Uses a lance weapon with long reach
- Can fly and attack from multiple angles
- Calls for reinforcement drones at health thresholds
- The fight has strategic depth requiring observation of attack patterns

### Mole Mines Boss: The Tunnel Wyrm

A massive creature that inhabits the deepest tunnels:
- Chase sequence through collapsing tunnels
- Must be fought in a confined arena
- Attacks include burrowing charges and rock throws
- Multiple weak points must be hit in sequence

## Recurring Characters Across Worlds

### The Traveling Merchant

A mysterious figure who appears in every world:
- Sells unique items not available from regular shopkeepers
- Inventory changes each time you encounter them
- Dialogue hints at knowing more about the world's situation than they let on
- Finding all merchant locations unlocks special dialogue

### The Sticker Collector

An enthusiastic NPC obsessed with [collecting stickers](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/):
- Appears in each world to track your sticker progress
- Offers rewards for collection milestones
- Provides hints about hidden sticker locations
- Their excitement grows as your collection expands

### The Medal Inspector

An official character who tracks your [medal progress](/blog/get-all-medals/):
- Found in each world's hub area
- Reviews which medals you have earned
- Suggests which medals to pursue next
- Awards special recognition for completing medal sets

## Character Interactions in Multiplayer

When playing in [multiplayer mode](/blog/multiplayer-guide/), NPC interactions have some differences:

- All players can speak to NPCs, but quest acceptance is typically host-dependent
- NPCs react to the presence of multiple bear characters with unique dialogue
- Some cooperative quests require multiple players to speak with different NPCs simultaneously
- NPC shopkeepers serve all players independently

## Using the Mod APK with Character Content

The [mod APK](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-apk-download/) provides access to all characters and cosmetics without the standard unlock requirements. With [unlimited money](/blog/super-bear-adventure-unlimited-money/), you can purchase any skin or hat immediately. The [mod menu](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-menu/) also offers options that affect character abilities, though the NPCs and story content remain unchanged.

## Conclusion

Super Bear Adventure's character roster brings the Kingdom of Earthkwak to life. Every NPC has personality, every enemy has purpose, and every boss tells a story. As the game continues to receive [updates](/blog/version-changelogs/), new characters are regularly added, expanding the world and enriching the player's experience.

Take the time to speak with every NPC, observe every enemy's behavior, and appreciate the thought that went into creating each character. The Kingdom of Earthkwak is made special not just by its environments but by the creatures who call it home.

All Missions in Super Bear Adventure - Complete Mission Guide (2026)
Complete guide to every mission in Super Bear Adventure. Find all missions across Turtletown, Snow Valley, Beemothep Desert, Giant House, and more with rewards listed.

## How Missions Work in Super Bear Adventure

![Game map overview showing all worlds](/images/gameplay/map-overview.svg)

Missions are the backbone of Super Bear Adventure. They drive your progression through each world, unlock new areas, and provide the coins and rewards you need to access everything the game offers. Understanding how the mission system works before diving into individual objectives saves time and prevents backtracking.

Every world in the game contains a set of main missions and optional side objectives. Main missions must be completed to advance the story and unlock access to the next world. Side missions are optional but reward you with coins, cosmetics, stickers, and other valuable items.

Missions are given by NPCs scattered throughout each world. Look for characters with markers above their heads. Talk to them to receive the objective, then complete the task and return to the same NPC for your reward. Some missions chain together, meaning completing one unlocks the next in a sequence.

Your mission log tracks active objectives and shows brief descriptions of what you need to do. Check it whenever you feel lost. If you are brand new to the game, our [beginner's guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) covers the fundamentals of controls and navigation before you start tackling missions.


## Mission Types Explained

Super Bear Adventure uses several mission archetypes that repeat across worlds with variations.

- **Fetch Missions:** Find and return specific items hidden in side paths, breakable objects, or guarded areas.
- **Defeat Missions:** Clear out a group of enemies from a specific area. Enemy count and types vary by world.
- **Escort Missions:** Guide an NPC safely from one point to another while fighting off threats.
- **Exploration Missions:** Reach a specific location through platforming or puzzle-solving.
- **Puzzle Missions:** Solve environmental puzzles by moving blocks, activating switches, or manipulating objects.
- **Boss Missions:** The final main mission in each world, pitting you against a major boss. Our [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) has detailed tactics.
- **Collection Missions:** Side objectives to gather collectibles like coins, stickers, or world-specific items.


## Turtletown Missions

![Turtletown village overview](/images/worlds/detail/turtletown-village.svg)

Turtletown is the first world and serves as your introduction to the game's mission structure. Difficulty is low, and the missions teach core mechanics. For a complete area walkthrough, see our [Turtletown walkthrough](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/).

### Main Missions

1. **Welcome to Turtletown** (Exploration) - Follow the path to the hilltop lookout. Teaches movement and camera controls. Reward: 50 coins.
2. **Turtle Trouble** (Defeat) - Defeat 10 turtles in the eastern meadow. Sidestep their charges and counter-attack. Reward: 75 coins.
3. **The Lost Shell** (Fetch) - Find the golden shell in the forest cave behind the waterfall east of the village. Double jump required. Reward: 100 coins + forest access.
4. **Bridge Builder** (Fetch) - Collect 5 wooden planks scattered around the village area to repair the northern bridge. Reward: 100 coins + northern access.
5. **The Village Elder's Request** (Escort) - Escort the elder to the hilltop shrine. Clear enemies ahead of him. Reward: 150 coins.
6. **King Turtle Showdown** (Boss) - Defeat King Turtle in three phases: straight charges, shell spins, and summoning minions. Attack during recovery windows. Reward: 200 coins + Snow Valley key.

### Side Missions

- **Coin Collector:** Gather 200 coins across Turtletown. Reward: Turtle Hat cosmetic.
- **Sticker Hunt:** Find all 8 stickers. Reward: 150 coins. See our [all secret stickers locations guide](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/).

## Best Order to Complete Missions

While the game gives you some freedom, following the intended progression path provides the smoothest experience in terms of difficulty scaling and story flow.

**Recommended progression:**

1. Complete all Turtletown main missions first. These teach mechanics you need everywhere else.
2. Do Turtletown side missions to build your coin reserve before moving on.
3. Progress through Snow Valley. The difficulty step up is manageable with Turtletown experience.
4. Tackle Beemothep Desert. Puzzle skills learned here help in Giant House.
5. Clear Giant House. The platforming challenges prepare you for The Hive.
6. Take on The Hive. Aerial combat training here pays off against the Mole King.
7. Finish with Mole Mines. This is the most demanding world and benefits from all prior experience.

**Within each world,** complete main missions in order since they often gate access to subsequent areas. Do side missions between main missions when you want a break from the critical path, or save them for a cleanup pass after finishing the world's boss.

## Tips for Difficult Missions

Some missions are significantly harder than others. Here are strategies for the most common sticking points.

**Timed missions (Dynamite Run, etc.):** Memorize the route first by running it without the objective. Once you know every turn and obstacle, the time pressure becomes manageable.

**Escort missions:** Clear enemies ahead of the NPC or cart rather than reacting to attacks. Proactive clearing prevents damage to what you are protecting.

**Stealth sections (Royal Jelly Heist):** Be patient. Wait for full guard rotation cycles before moving. Rushing leads to detection and heavy damage.

**Boss fights:** Every boss has a pattern. If you are dying repeatedly, spend an attempt just watching and dodging without attacking. Learn the full pattern, then use your next attempt to capitalize on vulnerability windows. The [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) provides all patterns.

**Platforming challenges (Bookshelf, Hive climbing):** Adjust your camera before every jump. A bad camera angle is the most common cause of missed platforms. Take your time, there is no rush penalty on platforming missions.

**Dark areas (Mole Mines):** Always carry a torch. Missing a torch pickup does not mean one is not available. Check wall brackets you may have passed. Some are hidden around corners.


## Frequently Asked Questions

### Can I replay completed missions?

Yes. You can replay any completed mission by talking to the original quest giver. Replayed missions reward reduced coins but are useful for practicing boss fights or finding missed collectibles.

### Do I have to complete side missions to progress?

No. Only main missions are required to unlock the next world. Side missions are entirely optional but provide valuable coins and unique cosmetic rewards.

### What happens if I fail a mission?

You respawn at the last checkpoint within the mission area. For timed missions, the timer resets. For escort missions, the NPC or cart returns to its starting position. You lose a life but keep any coins collected during the attempt.

### Are there any hidden missions not listed in the mission log?

Yes. Some NPCs offer missions only after you discover them in hidden locations. These are not tracked in the mission log until accepted. Exploring off the main paths frequently reveals these hidden quest givers. Our [secret areas and easter eggs guide](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/) documents their locations.

### Can I complete missions in multiplayer?

Most missions can be completed in co-op multiplayer. Boss missions and timed missions are generally more manageable with a partner. See the [multiplayer guide](/blog/multiplayer-guide/) for co-op mission tips.

### Which mission gives the most coins?

The Mole King boss fight rewards 500 coins, making it the single highest-paying mission. However, factoring in difficulty and time, the Crystal Cavern fetch mission (175 coins in about 5 minutes) offers one of the best coin-per-minute rates.

### Do mission rewards change in the mod version?

The mod version may adjust coin rewards or add bonus multipliers depending on the build. Check our [version changelogs](/blog/version-changelogs/) for mod-specific changes, and our [old versions archive](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-apk-old-versions/) if you need a specific build.

How Missions Work in Super Bear Adventure

Missions are the backbone of Super Bear Adventure. They drive your progression through each world, unlock new areas, and provide the coins and rewards you need to access everything the game offers. Understanding how the mission system works before diving into individual objectives saves time and prevents backtracking.

Every world in the game contains a set of main missions and optional side objectives. Main missions must be completed to advance the story and unlock access to the next world. Side missions are optional but reward you with coins, cosmetics, stickers, and other valuable items.

Missions are given by NPCs scattered throughout each world. Look for characters with markers above their heads. Talk to them to receive the objective, then complete the task and return to the same NPC for your reward. Some missions chain together, meaning completing one unlocks the next in a sequence.

Your mission log tracks active objectives and shows brief descriptions of what you need to do. Check it whenever you feel lost. If you are brand new to the game, our beginner’s guide covers the fundamentals of controls and navigation before you start tackling missions.

Mission Types Explained

Super Bear Adventure uses several mission archetypes that repeat across worlds with variations.

  • Fetch Missions: Find and return specific items hidden in side paths, breakable objects, or guarded areas.
  • Defeat Missions: Clear out a group of enemies from a specific area. Enemy count and types vary by world.
  • Escort Missions: Guide an NPC safely from one point to another while fighting off threats.
  • Exploration Missions: Reach a specific location through platforming or puzzle-solving.
  • Puzzle Missions: Solve environmental puzzles by moving blocks, activating switches, or manipulating objects.
  • Boss Missions: The final main mission in each world, pitting you against a major boss. Our boss fight strategy guide has detailed tactics.
  • Collection Missions: Side objectives to gather collectibles like coins, stickers, or world-specific items.

  6. King Turtle Showdown (Boss) - Defeat King Turtle in three phases: straight charges, shell spins, and summoning minions. Attack during recovery windows. Reward: 200 coins + Snow Valley key.

Arcade World Guide: All Mini-Games Explained & High Score Tips for Super Bear Adventure
Master every mini-game in Super Bear Adventure's Arcade World. Complete guide with rules, strategies, high score tips, and secret unlocks.

## Welcome to the Arcade World

The Arcade World is one of the most unique areas in Super Bear Adventure. Unlike the adventure-focused worlds like [Turtletown](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/) or [The Hive](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/), the Arcade World is a hub filled with playable mini-games, each offering a different genre of gameplay. From shooting galleries to racing challenges, the Arcade World provides a fun change of pace from the main game and rewards skilled players with coins, cosmetics, and even a secret sticker.

The Arcade World becomes accessible after completing at least two main worlds. Its entrance is located in the entertainment district of the overworld hub, marked by a neon sign and flashing lights. Inside, you will find a retro-styled arcade with multiple game cabinets, prize counters, and a few NPCs who provide tips and challenges.

This guide covers every mini-game in the Arcade World, explains the rules and scoring systems, provides strategies for achieving high scores, and reveals the secret content hidden within.

## Arcade World Layout

![Arcade World machines](/images/misc/arcade-machines.svg)

The Arcade World is organized into three main sections.

**The Main Floor** contains the standard mini-game cabinets. These are available from the moment you enter the Arcade World. There are five standard mini-games on the Main Floor.

**The VIP Section** is in the back of the arcade, behind a velvet rope. Access requires earning at least a silver rating in all five standard mini-games. The VIP Section contains two premium mini-games with higher difficulty and better rewards.

**The Secret Cabinet** is a hidden area that becomes accessible after earning gold ratings in all standard and VIP mini-games. It contains a unique retro mini-game and the Arcade World's boss encounter. For boss strategy details, see our [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/).

## Mini-Game Rating System

Each mini-game awards a rating based on your score. Ratings are bronze, silver, gold, and platinum. Bronze is awarded for completing the game at all, silver for reaching a moderate score threshold, gold for a high score, and platinum for a near-perfect performance. The specific score thresholds vary by game and are listed in each game's section below.

Ratings determine the coin rewards you receive. Bronze earns 20 coins, silver earns 50 coins, gold earns 100 coins, and platinum earns 200 coins. These coins are awarded each time you play, making high-rated mini-game runs a viable [coin farming method](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/).

## Standard Mini-Games

### 1. Target Blast

![Target Blast mini-game](/images/misc/arcade-target-blast.svg)

**Genre**: Shooting Gallery

**Rules**: Targets appear on a multi-lane range for 60 seconds. Tap or click on targets to shoot them. Regular targets are worth one point, golden targets are worth five points, and bomb targets subtract three points if hit. Targets appear for about two seconds before disappearing.

**Score Thresholds**: Bronze: 20 points. Silver: 50 points. Gold: 80 points. Platinum: 100 points.

**Strategy**: Focus on accuracy over speed. Missing a golden target is better than hitting a bomb target. Golden targets always appear in the same lanes: lanes two and four (counting from left). Regular targets alternate across all five lanes.

The target sequence follows a repeating pattern of 20 targets. After learning the pattern (three to four runs), you can anticipate where the next target will appear and pre-aim. The pattern is: lane 3 (regular), lane 1 (regular), lane 4 (golden), lane 2 (regular), lane 5 (bomb), lane 3 (regular), lane 2 (golden), lane 1 (regular), lane 4 (regular), lane 5 (regular), and then this repeats with slight variations.

For platinum, you need to hit virtually every non-bomb target including all golden targets. The window is tight but achievable with pattern memorization.

### 2. Whack-a-Mole

![Whack-a-Mole mini-game](/images/misc/arcade-whack-mole.svg)

**Genre**: Reflex Game

**Rules**: Moles pop out of nine holes arranged in a 3x3 grid. Moles stay up for about one and a half seconds. Hit moles for points. Regular moles are worth one point, golden moles are worth three points, and red moles subtract two points. Duration is 45 seconds.

**Score Thresholds**: Bronze: 15 points. Silver: 35 points. Gold: 55 points. Platinum: 70 points.

**Strategy**: Position yourself in the center of the grid so you have equal reach to all nine holes. The mole spawn pattern is semi-random but follows rules: golden moles only appear in corner holes, and red moles only appear in edge holes (not corners, not center). The center hole always spawns regular moles.

For high scores, prioritize golden moles in corners. They are worth triple and appear in more accessible positions. Ignore red moles entirely; it is better to miss a regular mole than to accidentally hit a red one while swinging.

The timing trick for platinum is to start moving toward a hole as soon as you see movement, not after the mole fully appears. The emerging animation is about half a second, giving you a head start.

### 3. Honey Slide Racing

![Honey Slide Racing](/images/misc/arcade-racing.svg)

**Genre**: Racing

**Rules**: Race through a honey slide course from [The Hive](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/) in the fastest time possible. The course has multiple paths, and choosing optimal routes reduces your time. Obstacles slow you down but do not end the run.

**Score Thresholds** (based on finish time): Bronze: under 60 seconds. Silver: under 45 seconds. Gold: under 35 seconds. Platinum: under 28 seconds.

**Strategy**: The course has three branching points. At each branch, the inside path (shorter distance) is faster despite having more obstacles. The time saved by the shorter distance outweighs the time lost to obstacle slowdowns.

For platinum, you need to take all three inside paths and avoid every obstacle on those paths. This requires memorizing the obstacle positions on the inside routes. The first inside path has two obstacles (duck under a beam, jump over a gap). The second has three obstacles (three successive jumps). The third has one obstacle (a tight turn that requires precise steering).

If you have played through The Hive world, the honey slide mechanics will feel familiar. If not, consider completing [The Hive walkthrough](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/) first to practice.

### 4. Cart Dash

**Genre**: Endless Runner

**Rules**: Ride a mine cart through a procedurally generated tunnel, collecting coins and avoiding obstacles. The run ends when you crash. Score is based on distance traveled plus coins collected. One point per meter of distance, one point per coin.

**Score Thresholds**: Bronze: 100 points. Silver: 300 points. Gold: 600 points. Platinum: 1,000 points.

**Strategy**: Unlike the scripted mine cart section in the [Mole Mines](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/), Cart Dash is procedurally generated, so you cannot memorize the layout. Instead, focus on reaction time and pattern recognition.

Obstacles come in three types: rocks (switch lanes), low beams (duck), and gaps (jump). They are always preceded by a visual warning at about two seconds of distance. Train yourself to react to the warning color: brown for rocks, gray for beams, black for gaps.

For high scores, prioritize survival over coin collection. Coins add to your score, but distance is the primary score driver. A run of 800 meters with few coins beats a run of 400 meters with many coins.

The speed gradually increases over time. The difficulty spike hits around 500 meters, where obstacles start appearing in combinations (jump then immediately duck). Be prepared to chain actions at this point.

### 5. Bear Boxing

**Genre**: Fighting

**Rules**: Fight against a series of bear opponents in a simplified boxing match. Each opponent has a health bar and a set of attacks. You can punch (fast, low damage), power punch (slow, high damage), and dodge (invulnerability frames). Win by reducing the opponent's health to zero.

**Score Thresholds** (based on opponents defeated): Bronze: 1 opponent. Silver: 3 opponents. Gold: 5 opponents. Platinum: 7 opponents (all opponents).

**Strategy**: Each opponent has a specific pattern. Opponent one only uses basic punches. Opponent two adds a power punch. Opponent three feints before attacking. Opponents four and five use combination attacks. Opponents six and seven have unique special moves.

The universal strategy is to dodge and counter-punch. Wait for the opponent to attack, dodge, then hit them with a power punch during their recovery. Trying to rush opponents with fast punches leads to trading damage, which is unsustainable against later opponents.

For platinum, you need to defeat all seven opponents without losing. Health does not regenerate between fights, so minimizing damage taken is critical. Master the dodge timing for each opponent's attack before moving to the next.

## VIP Mini-Games

### 6. Crystal Catcher

**Genre**: Puzzle

**Rules**: Colored crystals fall from the top of the screen. Catch them in matching colored baskets at the bottom. You control one basket at a time but can switch between four baskets (red, blue, green, yellow) with directional inputs. Catching a crystal in the right basket earns points. Wrong basket loses a life (three lives total). Duration is 90 seconds or until lives run out.

**Score Thresholds**: Bronze: 30 points. Silver: 60 points. Gold: 100 points. Platinum: 140 points.

**Strategy**: Crystals fall in waves of four, one of each color. The fall speed is constant but the order is randomized. The key skill is quick basket switching, as you often need to switch baskets multiple times within a single wave.

Position yourself under the crystal that will land first (the one closest to the bottom when the wave spawns) with the matching basket. As soon as you catch it, switch to the next basket and move to the next crystal.

For platinum, you need to catch almost every crystal correctly for the full 90 seconds. The margin for error is about five missed crystals. Practice the basket-switching motion until it is muscle memory.

### 7. Tower Defense

**Genre**: Strategy

**Rules**: Enemies march along a path toward your base. Place bear defender units along the path to stop them. Units cost coins earned during the round. Different unit types have different attack ranges, speeds, and damage. Survive ten waves to complete the game.

**Score Thresholds** (based on remaining base health): Bronze: survive with any health. Silver: survive with 50 percent health. Gold: survive with 80 percent health. Platinum: survive with 100 percent health.

**Strategy**: The three unit types are: Boxer Bear (cheap, short range, moderate damage), Archer Bear (medium cost, long range, low damage), and Bomb Bear (expensive, area damage, slow attack).

The optimal layout is Archer Bears at the first and third bends of the path (long sightlines), Boxer Bears at the second bend (high traffic area), and Bomb Bears at the final bend before the base (emergency area damage for groups).

For platinum, start with two Archer Bears at the first bend. Save coins until wave three, then place Boxer Bears. Bomb Bears should only be placed after wave five when enemy groups start clumping. Upgrade units when possible rather than placing new ones.

## The Secret Cabinet: Bear Invaders

**Genre**: Retro Shooter

**Rules**: A Space Invaders parody. Rows of enemy bears descend from the top of the screen. You control a cannon at the bottom, moving left and right and shooting upward. Enemies fire back. Clear all enemies to complete a wave. The game has five increasingly difficult waves.

**Score Thresholds**: Bronze: clear wave 1. Silver: clear wave 3. Gold: clear wave 5. Platinum: clear wave 5 without losing a life.

**Strategy**: This plays exactly like classic Space Invaders with a few twists. Some enemy bears have shields that require two hits. The boss bear at the end of wave five has a health bar and fires homing projectiles.

Stay near the center of the screen for maximum coverage. Move to the edges only to pick off corner enemies. Focus on one side of the formation to create gaps that reduce incoming fire density.

For the wave five boss, stay directly below it. Its attacks are aimed to the sides, so the safest position is actually directly underneath. Fire continuously and dodge the occasional straight-down shot.

## Rewards and Unlocks

### Coin Rewards

Mini-games are a reliable source of coins. A skilled player running platinum-level Target Blast and Whack-a-Mole can earn 400 coins in about 15 minutes. This competes with other [coin farming methods](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/) and has the advantage of variety.

### Sticker Unlock

The Arcade World sticker (Sticker 15) is awarded for achieving a perfect score in any one standard mini-game. The easiest game to perfect is Whack-a-Mole, as its patterns are the most predictable. See our [sticker locations guide](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/) for details on this and all other stickers.

### Cosmetic Rewards

Earning gold ratings in all seven standard and VIP mini-games unlocks the Arcade Bear cosmetic hat, a joystick-shaped headpiece. Earning platinum in all games unlocks the Retro Visor accessory.

### The Glitch Boss

Completing Bear Invaders in the Secret Cabinet triggers the appearance of The Glitch, the Arcade World's boss. This is an optional boss fight and one of the hardest in the game. Our [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) covers the full encounter.

## Tips for Improving Mini-Game Performance

### Practice Mode

Each mini-game has a practice mode accessible by interacting with the cabinet and selecting "Practice" instead of "Play." Practice mode does not award ratings or coins but lets you play without score pressure, perfect for learning patterns and timing.

### Controller vs Touch

If you are playing on mobile, some mini-games are easier with a connected controller, especially Cart Dash and Bear Boxing where precise timing matters. If you are playing on PC, check our [PC guide](/blog/super-bear-adventure-for-pc-guide/) for control setup recommendations.

### Performance Impact

The Arcade World's visual effects (neon lights, particle effects) can cause performance issues on lower-end devices. Frame drops during mini-games directly impact your reaction time and scores. If you experience lag, our [lag fix guide](/blog/lag-fix-optimization/) can help optimize performance.

### Study the Leaderboards

The high score boards in the Arcade World show the best scores. While the default entries are developer-set placeholders, they represent the approximate score needed for platinum. Use them as benchmarks for your own performance.

## Arcade World Easter Eggs

The Arcade World is filled with fun details and [easter eggs](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/). The broken cabinet in the corner reveals game statistics if interacted with five times. The high score initials spell out "BEARS RULE" when read vertically. Posters on the walls reference other games by the same developer. And the floor tiles form a pixel art bear face when viewed from the VIP Section's elevated platform.

## Conclusion

The Arcade World is a fantastic addition to Super Bear Adventure that provides hours of entertainment beyond the main adventure. Each mini-game offers a unique challenge, and the progression from bronze through platinum gives every player something to strive for. Whether you are farming coins, hunting for the sticker, or trying to conquer The Glitch, the Arcade World delivers variety and fun.

For more Super Bear Adventure content, explore our world guides for [The Hive](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/), [Mole Mines](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/), and other worlds. If you are working toward 100 percent completion, our guides for [all sticker locations](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/) and [all skins and hats](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/) complement the Arcade World's rewards. And if you run into any issues with the Arcade World not loading or crashing, our [troubleshooting guide](/blog/troubleshooting-common-errors/) has solutions.

All 15 Secret Sticker Locations in Super Bear Adventure: Complete Collection Guide
Find all 15 secret stickers in Super Bear Adventure with exact locations, step-by-step directions, and screenshots for every world.

## What Are Secret Stickers?

Secret stickers are one of the most sought-after collectibles in Super Bear Adventure. There are 15 stickers hidden across every world in the game, and collecting all of them unlocks the exclusive Golden Bear skin, one of the rarest cosmetics available. Unlike coins and gems that are scattered abundantly throughout levels, stickers are carefully hidden in secret areas, behind puzzles, and in locations that most players walk right past.

Each sticker is a unique design featuring characters, items, and scenes from the game's lore. They appear as small glowing cards floating in the air, and collecting one triggers a brief animation showing the sticker being added to your collection book. You can view your collected stickers in the pause menu under the "Collection" tab.

If you are just getting started with Super Bear Adventure, check out our [beginner guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) to learn the basic mechanics before hunting for stickers. Many sticker locations require advanced movement techniques like wall jumping, precise platforming, or knowledge of hidden mechanics.

## Sticker Collection Overview

![Sticker collection album](/images/items/sticker-collection.svg)

The 15 stickers are distributed across the game's worlds as follows:

- **Overworld Hub**: 2 stickers
- **Turtletown**: 2 stickers
- **Snow Valley**: 2 stickers
- **Beemothep Desert**: 2 stickers
- **The Hive**: 2 stickers
- **Mole Mines**: 2 stickers
- **Giant House**: 2 stickers
- **Arcade World**: 1 sticker

Each world section below provides the exact location, how to reach it, and any prerequisites needed.

## Overworld Hub Stickers

### Sticker 1: Behind the Waterfall

**Location**: The main waterfall near the center of the overworld hub.

**How to Find It**: From the spawn point, head north toward the large waterfall that flows into the river. Instead of following the river, walk directly into the waterfall itself. Behind the water curtain, there is a narrow ledge leading to a small cave. The sticker floats at the back of this cave.

**Prerequisites**: None. This sticker is accessible from the very start of the game, making it a great first find.

**Difficulty**: Easy.

### Sticker 2: Top of the Clocktower

**Location**: The clocktower building in the village area of the overworld hub.

**How to Find It**: The clocktower has no obvious way to climb it from the inside. Instead, go to the building next to the clocktower (the item shop) and climb the ladder on its back wall to reach the shop's roof. From the roof, perform a running jump to the clocktower's ledge. Shimmy around the ledge to the back side, where a series of small platforms leads to the top. The sticker is on the very top of the clocktower, next to the clock face.

**Prerequisites**: You need to understand the wall-shimmy mechanic. Hold the direction toward the wall while on a narrow ledge to shimmy along it.

**Difficulty**: Medium.

## Turtletown Stickers

![Turtletown sticker locations](/images/worlds/detail/turtletown-collectibles.svg)

### Sticker 3: Underwater Cave

**Location**: Turtletown's main beach area, underwater.

**How to Find It**: Swim out from the main beach into the ocean. Dive underwater and look for a dark opening in the sea floor near the third coral formation from the shore. Swim down into the opening to enter an underwater cave system. Follow the cave to the right, and you will surface in a small air pocket chamber. The sticker is floating above the water in this chamber.

**Prerequisites**: Familiarity with the underwater swimming controls. Your air meter is not a concern here, as the cave is short enough to reach the air pocket comfortably.

**Difficulty**: Medium.

For the full Turtletown walkthrough including all secrets, see our [Turtletown guide](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/).

### Sticker 4: Palm Tree Canopy

**Location**: The group of tall palm trees in Turtletown's village area.

**How to Find It**: Near the turtle NPC village, there is a cluster of five tall palm trees growing close together. Climb the shortest palm tree using the grab-and-climb mechanic (jump onto the trunk and hold up). At the top, jump from the first tree to the second, then to the third. The third tree has wider fronds that form a small platform. The sticker sits on this platform, hidden from view at ground level.

**Prerequisites**: Comfortable with the tree-climbing mechanic and tree-to-tree jumping.

**Difficulty**: Medium.

## Snow Valley Stickers

### Sticker 5: Inside the Frozen Lake

**Location**: Snow Valley's large frozen lake.

**How to Find It**: Walk onto the frozen lake and look for a spot where the ice is darker and slightly transparent. This is near the center of the lake, slightly to the left. Ground pound on this dark ice patch three times to crack it open. Fall through the hole into a hidden ice cave beneath the lake. The sticker is in this cave along with a coin bonus.

**Prerequisites**: Ground pound ability. The ice does not break from normal walking or jumping.

**Difficulty**: Easy once you know the location.

For more Snow Valley secrets, visit our [Snow Valley secrets guide](/blog/snow-valley-secrets/).

### Sticker 6: Mountain Peak Secret

**Location**: The highest mountain peak in Snow Valley.

**How to Find It**: The tallest mountain in Snow Valley appears to be just scenery, but it is actually climbable. Start from the back side of the mountain (the side facing away from the main path). A series of narrow ledges and ice platforms create a climbing path that spirals around the mountain. The climb takes about two minutes and includes several tricky jumps across icy platforms that you can slide off. At the absolute peak, the sticker hovers above a small flat area.

**Prerequisites**: Patience and precision platforming. The icy surfaces reduce traction, so tap the directional input rather than holding it.

**Difficulty**: Hard.

## Beemothep Desert Stickers

### Sticker 7: Sphinx Puzzle Reward

**Location**: Inside the Sphinx structure in the Beemothep Desert.

**How to Find It**: The Sphinx has a riddle puzzle. Approach the front of the Sphinx and interact with the stone tablet at its base. The tablet presents three riddles. The answers correspond to items you need to bring from around the desert. Answer one: a red flower (found near the oasis). Answer two: a bone (found near the fossil area). Answer three: a gold coin (any coin from your inventory counts). After solving all three riddles, a chamber opens inside the Sphinx containing the sticker.

**Prerequisites**: Exploration of the Beemothep Desert to find the puzzle items.

**Difficulty**: Medium.

See the [Beemothep Desert puzzle guide](/blog/beemothep-desert-puzzle/) for a full breakdown of all the desert's puzzles.

### Sticker 8: Sand Dune Tunnel

**Location**: A hidden tunnel beneath the largest sand dune in the desert.

**How to Find It**: The largest sand dune is in the northwestern part of the desert map. Walk to the base of its eastern face and look for a small, dark opening partially covered by sand. Crouch to enter the tunnel. Inside, the tunnel winds downward into a small underground chamber decorated with ancient symbols. The sticker is on a pedestal in the center of this chamber.

**Prerequisites**: Crouch ability. The entrance is easy to miss because it blends with the surrounding sand.

**Difficulty**: Easy once located, but finding the entrance is the challenge.

## The Hive Stickers

### Sticker 9: Honeycomb Corridors Breakable Wall

**Location**: The Honeycomb Corridors area of The Hive.

**How to Find It**: In the second set of corridors, look for a wall section that has a slightly different shade of gold compared to its neighbors. Punch this wall three times to break through it, revealing a small room with the sticker and a coin bonus.

**Prerequisites**: None beyond basic combat controls.

**Difficulty**: Easy.

Full details on The Hive's layout and secrets are in our [Hive walkthrough](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/).

### Sticker 10: Royal Jelly Chamber False Floor

**Location**: The Royal Jelly Chamber in The Hive, on the third platform while spiraling upward.

**How to Find It**: As you ascend the Royal Jelly Chamber, the third hexagonal platform you reach has a tile that is very slightly raised compared to the others. Step on this raised tile to trigger a trapdoor that drops you into a hidden room below. The sticker is in this room along with 75 coins. Use the honey wall inside the room to climb back to the main chamber.

**Prerequisites**: Honey wall climbing ability (learned in The Hive's first area).

**Difficulty**: Medium. Noticing the raised tile requires attention to detail.

## Mole Mines Stickers

### Sticker 11: Upper Tunnels Crystal Cave

**Location**: A side tunnel in the Upper Tunnels of the Mole Mines.

**How to Find It**: In the Upper Tunnels' first large cavern, there is a side tunnel branching to the left that is partially hidden behind a rock formation. Follow this tunnel to reach a small crystal cave. The sticker is floating among the crystal formations.

**Prerequisites**: Defeat the group of digger moles guarding the tunnel entrance.

**Difficulty**: Easy.

See our [Mole Mines walkthrough](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/) for a complete guide to this world.

### Sticker 12: Mine Cart Secret Track

**Location**: During the Mine Cart Express section of the Mole Mines.

**How to Find It**: About halfway through the cart ride, during the chasm section, look for a side track branching to the right just before the second gap. Instead of jumping the gap, switch to the right track. This side track leads to a hidden room where the sticker awaits.

**Prerequisites**: Familiarity with the mine cart controls. You need to know the track layout to time the switch correctly. The branch point comes up fast.

**Difficulty**: Hard. The timing is tight and you only get one chance per run.

## Giant House Stickers

### Sticker 13: Inside the Dollhouse

**Location**: The dollhouse on the shelf in the Giant House world.

**How to Find It**: In the Giant House, climb the bookshelf near the window. On the third shelf, there is a dollhouse. Your bear is small enough to enter through the dollhouse's front door. Inside, the dollhouse is a miniature level with a few simple platforming challenges. The sticker is in the dollhouse's attic, reached by climbing the dollhouse's interior stairs.

**Prerequisites**: Ability to reach the third shelf of the bookshelf via platforming.

**Difficulty**: Medium.

For more on the Giant House world, read the [Giant House walkthrough](/blog/giant-house-walkthrough/).

### Sticker 14: Behind the Clock

**Location**: The large wall clock in the Giant House.

**How to Find It**: The giant wall clock in the living room area can be reached by jumping from the mantelpiece. Walk along the narrow ledge above the fireplace and jump to the clock's lower edge. Climb around to the back of the clock (the wall side has a small gap between the clock and the wall). Behind the clock, there is a tiny ledge with the sticker.

**Prerequisites**: Precise jumping and ledge navigation. This is one of the more challenging platforming sticker locations.

**Difficulty**: Hard.

## Arcade World Sticker

### Sticker 15: Perfect Score Reward

**Location**: Awarded as a reward in the Arcade World.

**How to Find It**: This is the only sticker that is not found by exploring a hidden location. Instead, you earn it by achieving a perfect score in any one of the Arcade World's mini-games. The easiest mini-game to perfect is the Whack-a-Mole game, as its patterns are predictable once you memorize them. Achieving a perfect score on any game awards the sticker.

**Prerequisites**: Access to the [Arcade World](/blog/arcade-world-mini-games/) and skill at mini-games.

**Difficulty**: Medium to Hard depending on the mini-game chosen.

## What Happens When You Collect All 15 Stickers

Collecting all 15 stickers completes your sticker collection book and triggers a special cutscene where your bear is awarded the Golden Bear skin. This is one of the most prestigious cosmetics in the game, as it requires thorough exploration of every world. The Golden Bear skin makes your entire bear model a shimmering gold color and adds a subtle sparkle particle effect.

In addition to the skin, completing the collection unlocks the "Sticker Master" achievement and adds a special icon next to your name in [multiplayer mode](/blog/multiplayer-guide/). Other players can see that you have completed the collection, which is a mark of dedication and skill.

For a complete list of all cosmetics including the Golden Bear skin, visit our [skins and hats guide](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/).

## Sticker Hunting Tips

![Map with sticker markers](/images/gameplay/map-overview.svg)

### Use the Sticker Radar

After collecting your fifth sticker, you unlock the Sticker Radar item, which appears as a compass-like indicator on your HUD when you are in a world that has uncollected stickers. The radar pulses faster as you get closer to a sticker, functioning like a hot-cold indicator. This makes finding the remaining stickers much easier.

### Revisit Worlds

You can replay any world at any time from the world select menu. Stickers you have already collected will not appear again, so you can focus on searching new areas without confusion. The world select menu also shows how many stickers you have found in each world versus the total.

### Check Behind Everything

The developers of Super Bear Adventure love hiding stickers behind objects, inside containers, and in areas that require you to look at the environment from unusual angles. Walk behind every large object, enter every doorway, and test every suspicious surface. Breakable walls are common sticker hiding spots, and our [secret areas guide](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/) covers additional hidden areas beyond sticker locations.

### Listen for Audio Cues

When you are near a sticker, a faint chiming sound plays. This audio cue is subtle but distinct from other game sounds. If you hear chiming while exploring, stop and search the immediate area carefully. The sound gets louder as you get closer.

## Conclusion

Finding all 15 secret stickers in Super Bear Adventure is a rewarding challenge that encourages you to explore every corner of the game's beautifully crafted worlds. From the underwater caves of Turtletown to the glitching arenas of the Arcade World, each sticker is hidden with creativity and care.

Use this guide to track down every sticker, earn the Golden Bear skin, and achieve 100 percent completion. And if you run into any issues while playing, our [troubleshooting guide](/blog/troubleshooting-common-errors/) covers common problems and solutions.

What Are Secret Stickers?

Secret stickers are one of the most sought-after collectibles in Super Bear Adventure. There are 15 stickers hidden across every world in the game, and collecting all of them unlocks the exclusive Golden Bear skin, one of the rarest cosmetics available. Unlike coins and gems that are scattered abundantly throughout levels, stickers are carefully hidden in secret areas, behind puzzles, and in locations that most players walk right past.

Each sticker is a unique design featuring characters, items, and scenes from the game’s lore. They appear as small glowing cards floating in the air, and collecting one triggers a brief animation showing the sticker being added to your collection book. You can view your collected stickers in the pause menu under the “Collection” tab.

If you are just getting started with Super Bear Adventure, check out our beginner guide to learn the basic mechanics before hunting for stickers. Many sticker locations require advanced movement techniques like wall jumping, precise platforming, or knowledge of hidden mechanics.

## Introduction to Beemothep Desert

Beemothep Desert is one of the most atmospheric and puzzle-heavy worlds in Super Bear Adventure. Unlike the lush greenery of Turtletown or the icy terrain of Snow Valley, Beemothep Desert drops you into a vast expanse of scorching sand, ancient ruins, and hidden tombs. The world is packed with environmental puzzles, collectible secrets, and one of the most memorable boss encounters in the entire game.

What makes Beemothep Desert stand out from other worlds is the sheer density of its puzzle design. Nearly every landmark in the desert serves a purpose, from the towering Sphinx at the entrance to the crumbling pyramids scattered across the dunes. If you are the kind of player who enjoys exploration and problem-solving over pure platforming, this world was built for you.

This guide covers absolutely everything you need to know about Beemothep Desert. We will walk through every puzzle solution, every secret sticker location, every treasure chest, and the strategies you need to defeat the desert boss. If you are brand new to the game, we recommend reading our [beginner guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) first to get familiar with the core mechanics before tackling this challenging world.

## How to Access Beemothep Desert

![Great pyramid entrance](/images/worlds/detail/desert-pyramid.svg)

Before you can explore the desert, you need to unlock it. Beemothep Desert is not available from the very start of the game. Here is what you need to do:

1. **Complete the Turtletown main missions.** You need to finish at least the primary story missions in Turtletown before the desert becomes accessible. Our [Turtletown walkthrough](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/) covers everything you need to complete there.
2. **Collect enough stickers.** You will need a minimum sticker count to open the portal to Beemothep Desert. The exact requirement can vary depending on your game version, but generally you need around 15 to 20 stickers.
3. **Find the desert portal.** The portal to Beemothep Desert is located in the main hub world. Look for an archway decorated with golden sand textures and hieroglyphic patterns near the eastern side of the hub area.
4. **Enter and confirm.** Walk through the portal and confirm the world transition. You will spawn at the Desert Oasis, which serves as your starting point and safe zone.

If you are having trouble locating the portal or meeting the sticker requirements, check our [all secret stickers locations](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/) guide for help collecting more stickers quickly.

## Navigating the Desert Terrain

Beemothep Desert introduces several environmental mechanics that do not appear in other worlds. Understanding these mechanics is essential before you start tackling puzzles.

### Heat and Shade Mechanics

The desert sun is not just cosmetic. When you are out in the open sand for extended periods, a heat meter gradually fills up. If the heat meter maxes out, you start losing health slowly. To manage this:

- **Stay in shaded areas.** Rock overhangs, palm trees near oases, and the interiors of pyramids all provide shade that resets your heat meter.
- **Use water sources.** Stepping into any pool of water or the main oasis instantly cools you down and resets the heat gauge completely.
- **Move efficiently.** Plan your routes between shaded landmarks rather than wandering aimlessly across open dunes.

### Sand Movement

Walking on sand is slower than walking on solid ground. Your movement speed is reduced by roughly 20 to 30 percent when traversing deep sand areas. This matters more than you might think during timed puzzles and boss encounters. Look for stone pathways and rocky outcrops where you can move at full speed.

### Quicksand Zones

Several areas in Beemothep Desert contain quicksand patches. These are visually distinct from regular sand because they have a slightly darker color and a subtle rippling animation. If you step into quicksand, you will start sinking and take damage. To cross quicksand safely:

- **Jump across.** Short quicksand patches can be cleared with well-timed jumps.
- **Use stepping stones.** Most large quicksand areas have small rock platforms or debris you can hop across.
- **Look for hidden bridges.** Some quicksand sections have invisible or buried platforms that become visible when you get close. Walk along the edges and watch for visual cues.
- **Roll through.** If you have unlocked the roll ability, you can sometimes roll across quicksand faster than it can pull you down.

## The Sphinx and Its Riddles

![Sphinx puzzle monument](/images/worlds/detail/desert-sphinx.svg)

The Sphinx is the centerpiece of Beemothep Desert and the key to unlocking the most valuable rewards in the world. This massive stone structure sits at the center of the desert, and you will need to interact with it multiple times throughout your exploration.

### How the Sphinx Puzzles Work

When you approach the Sphinx and interact with it, you will be presented with a riddle or puzzle challenge. The Sphinx has a total of five puzzle stages, each more difficult than the last. Solving each stage unlocks a new area of the desert or grants a valuable reward. You do not need to solve all five in a single visit. You can return to the Sphinx at any time to attempt the next stage.

### Sphinx Puzzle 1: The Symbol Match

The first puzzle presents you with a grid of hieroglyphic symbols. You need to match pairs of identical symbols by stepping on pressure plates in the correct order.

**Solution:** Start with the plates in the corners. The matching pairs are always arranged symmetrically. Work from the outside in, matching corner symbols first, then edge symbols, and finally the center pair. The pattern does not change between attempts, so if you fail, you can retry with the same solution.

### Sphinx Puzzle 2: The Light Beam Redirect

The second puzzle involves redirecting beams of light using movable mirrors. You need to guide a beam of sunlight from the entrance of the Sphinx chamber to a crystal at the back of the room.

**Solution:**

1. Push the first mirror one space to the right so the beam reflects toward the north wall.
2. Rotate the second mirror by interacting with it twice to angle the beam east.
3. Move the third mirror to the raised platform using the nearby ramp, then rotate it once to send the beam into the crystal.
4. The crystal activates, and a door opens to the next chamber.

The trick here is that you need to push the mirrors in the correct sequence. If you move the third mirror before the second, the beam path gets blocked by a pillar.

### Sphinx Puzzle 3: The Pressure Plate Sequence

This puzzle features a room full of pressure plates, and you must step on them in the correct order. The order is hinted at by murals on the walls.

**Solution:** Read the wall murals from left to right. Each mural depicts an animal, and the corresponding pressure plate has the same animal symbol engraved on it. The correct order is: Scarab, Snake, Eagle, Cat, Bear. Step on each plate in this order without touching any wrong plate. Stepping on a wrong plate resets the puzzle and spawns a wave of enemies.

### Sphinx Puzzle 4: The Water Flow Puzzle

The fourth challenge involves redirecting water through a series of channels to fill a basin. You interact with valve wheels to open and close different channel sections.

**Solution:**

1. Open Valve A (the one closest to the water source on the left side).
2. Close Valve B to block the water from flowing into the drainage pit.
3. Open Valve C to redirect the flow toward the central basin.
4. Wait for the basin to fill completely. This takes about 15 seconds of real time.
5. Once full, a platform rises from the basin, giving you access to the reward chamber above.

### Sphinx Puzzle 5: The Final Trial

The final Sphinx puzzle is a combination of platforming and puzzle-solving. You must navigate a vertical chamber with disappearing platforms while activating switches in the correct order.

**Solution:** There are four switches, colored red, blue, yellow, and green. Activate them in this order: Blue, Yellow, Red, Green. Between each switch, you need to jump across disappearing platforms. The platforms follow a consistent rhythm, appearing for about two seconds and disappearing for one second. Time your jumps to the rhythm and do not rush. After activating all four switches, the top of the chamber opens to reveal the Sphinx Treasure Room.

## Pyramid Exploration

Beemothep Desert contains three pyramids, each with its own interior layout and set of challenges. These are some of the most rewarding areas to explore in the entire game.

### The Small Pyramid (Western Dunes)

This is the easiest pyramid and the one most players discover first. It is located in the western section of the desert near a cluster of palm trees.

- **Interior layout:** Two floors with simple corridor navigation.
- **Enemies:** Mummy enemies that patrol hallways. They move slowly but hit hard.
- **Treasure:** One treasure chest containing coins and one secret sticker on the second floor behind a breakable wall.
- **Tip:** Bring the mummy enemies near the torches on the walls. They are weak to fire and will be defeated in one hit if they touch a torch flame.

### The Medium Pyramid (Central Desert)

Located near the Sphinx, this pyramid has more complex navigation and a trap-filled interior.

- **Interior layout:** Three floors with multiple branching paths, spike traps, and rolling boulder hazards.
- **Enemies:** Mummy enemies and sand scorpions that emerge from the floor.
- **Treasure:** Two treasure chests and one secret sticker hidden in a chamber accessed by a hidden switch on the second floor.
- **Tip:** The rolling boulders follow a set path. Stand in the alcoves along the corridors and wait for them to pass before moving to the next safe spot.

### The Grand Pyramid (Eastern Cliffs)

The largest and most dangerous pyramid. This is where advanced players will find the biggest rewards. If you find the combat here too difficult, check our [boss fight strategy](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) guide for general combat tips that apply to these tougher enemies.

- **Interior layout:** Five floors with a mix of puzzles, combat encounters, and platforming sections.
- **Enemies:** Elite mummies, sand scorpions, and a mini-boss on the fourth floor.
- **Treasure:** Three treasure chests, two secret stickers, and a unique cosmetic item (the Pharaoh Hat) in the top chamber.
- **Tip:** The mini-boss on the fourth floor is a giant scarab beetle. It charges in straight lines, so dodge to the side and attack its exposed back when it crashes into a wall.

## Hidden Tomb Areas

Beyond the three main pyramids, Beemothep Desert has several hidden tombs scattered throughout the landscape. These are easy to miss if you are not actively exploring.

### Tomb of the Sand King

Located beneath a seemingly ordinary sand dune in the northern desert. Look for a ring of small stones on the ground. Stand in the center and perform a ground pound to break through into the tomb below.

- Contains a lengthy corridor with wall-mounted dart traps.
- Reward: A treasure chest with a large coin haul and a rare sticker.

### The Buried Temple

Found in the southeastern corner of the map, partially buried under sand. You can spot the entrance by looking for a stone doorframe sticking out of a dune.

- Features a single large puzzle room with rotating pillars that you must align to form a complete image.
- Reward: Access to a hidden oasis below the temple with a unique collectible.

### The Scarab Vault

This tomb only becomes accessible after completing Sphinx Puzzle 3. A door opens in the cliff face near the Sphinx revealing a downward staircase.

- Contains the largest single coin deposit in the entire desert.
- Also holds one of the rarest stickers in the game.

3. **Medium Pyramid Sticker:** Hidden chamber on the second floor of the Medium Pyramid. Find the switch disguised as a wall torch.

5. **Grand Pyramid Sticker 2:** Top chamber of the Grand Pyramid, behind the Pharaoh Hat pedestal.

8. **Scarab Vault Sticker:** Inside the Scarab Vault, which opens after solving Sphinx Puzzle 3.

## Treasure Chest Locations

There are twelve treasure chests spread across Beemothep Desert. Most contain coins, but some hold unique items.

- **Chest 1:** Behind the waterfall at the starting oasis.

## The Desert Boss Fight

![Sand Pharaoh boss fight](/images/worlds/detail/desert-boss.svg)

The Beemothep Desert boss is the Sand Wyrm, a massive serpentine creature that burrows through the sand and attacks from below. This is one of the harder boss fights in Super Bear Adventure and requires patience and pattern recognition.

### Phase 1: Sand Eruptions

The Sand Wyrm stays underground and periodically erupts from the sand at your location. Watch for circular sand disturbances on the ground, which indicate where the wyrm will emerge. Move away from the disturbance and wait for the wyrm to surface. When it does, it will be briefly stunned. Attack its head during this window.

### Phase 2: Surface Chase

After taking enough damage, the Sand Wyrm begins circling the arena on the surface. It moves fast and leaves a trail of quicksand behind it. Stay on the stone platforms in the arena and wait for it to pass close enough to attack. Three to four hits during this phase will push it to the final phase.

### Phase 3: Desperation Attacks

The Sand Wyrm becomes more aggressive, combining sand eruptions with surface charges and summoning smaller sand scorpion minions. Focus on avoiding the eruptions while clearing the scorpions when you have safe moments. The wyrm will periodically pause to roar, which gives you an attack window. Four to five hits during these pauses will finish the fight.

### Boss Tips

- Equip any speed-boosting skins or hats before the fight. Movement speed is critical for dodging sand eruptions. Check our [unlock all skins and hats](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/) guide for options.
- Stock up on coins beforehand for any revival or power-up purchases. Our [unlimited coins farming](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/) guide has efficient methods.
- Do not try to chase the wyrm during Phase 2. Let it come to you.
- Clear the scorpion minions in Phase 3 immediately. They can overwhelm you if ignored.

## Oasis Secrets

![Desert oasis location](/images/worlds/detail/desert-oasis.svg)

The starting oasis is more than just a safe zone. It contains several hidden areas and secrets that many players walk right past.

- **Hidden cave behind the waterfall:** Walk through the waterfall to find a small cave with a treasure chest and a coin deposit.
- **Underwater passage:** Dive into the deepest part of the oasis pool and look for a tunnel on the east side. It leads to a small underground chamber with a collectible.
- **Palm tree tops:** Several palm trees around the oasis can be climbed. The tallest one has a coin ring at the top and offers a vantage point where you can spot other secrets in the distance.

## Ancient Ruins Exploration

The ancient ruins are located in the central-eastern part of the desert and consist of crumbling stone structures, broken pillars, and partially buried walls. While they may look like simple scenery, the ruins contain some of the best rewards in the area.

- **Column jumping puzzle:** Jump across the tops of the broken pillars to reach a high platform with a treasure chest.
- **Hidden basement:** One of the ruined buildings has a floor that can be ground-pounded to reveal a basement with enemies and loot.
- **Hieroglyphic wall:** A wall in the ruins displays hieroglyphics that provide hints for the Sphinx puzzles. If you are stuck on a Sphinx challenge, come here for clues.

## Coin Farming Spots in Beemothep Desert

Beemothep Desert offers some of the best coin farming opportunities in the game, especially if you know where to look.

1. **Oasis coin circuit:** A loop around the oasis collects around 50 coins per run and takes about 30 seconds. The coins respawn after you leave and re-enter the area.
2. **Small Pyramid speed run:** Clearing the Small Pyramid from entrance to exit takes about two minutes and yields roughly 150 coins including enemy drops.
3. **Ancient ruins enemies:** The enemies in the ruins respawn frequently and drop good coin amounts. A five-minute farming session here can net 300 or more coins.
4. **Scarab Vault:** Once unlocked, this single room contains the largest coin deposit in the desert at around 500 coins.

For more advanced farming techniques across all worlds, see our dedicated [unlimited coins farming](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/) guide.
