## Frequently Asked Questions

### How long does it take to complete Giant House?

A focused playthrough covering all main missions takes approximately 45 minutes to one hour. Finding every secret and collectible can extend that to two hours or more.

### What is the hardest part of Giant House?

Most players find the ceiling fan challenge in the living room to be the most difficult platforming section. The kitchen stovetop crossing and the attic spider encounters are also commonly cited as challenging areas.

### Can I leave Giant House and come back later?

Yes. All progress within Giant House is saved. You can return to the hub world at any time through the mouse hole entrance and come back to pick up where you left off.

### Do I need to complete Giant House to access later worlds?

Giant House completion contributes to your overall sticker count and mission progress, which may be required for accessing later worlds. However, the specific requirements vary. Check our [all missions guide](/blog/all-missions-guide/) for the full progression path.

### The game runs slowly in Giant House. How can I fix it?

Giant House has more complex geometry than most worlds due to the detailed room environments. If you experience lag, our [lag fix and optimization](/blog/lag-fix-optimization/) guide has specific settings recommendations. Lowering shadow quality and reducing particle effects will have the biggest impact in this world.

### I cannot find the basement key. Where is it?

The basement key is under the doormat at the front door in the main hallway. You need to ground-pound the doormat to reveal it. It is one of the first things available in the world, but many players overlook it.

### Is there a way to fast travel between rooms?

There is no formal fast travel system within Giant House. However, the shortcut passage behind the umbrella stand in the hallway connects directly to the kitchen, and the bathroom pipe passage connects to the basement. These shortcuts save significant travel time once discovered.

## Final Thoughts on Giant House

Giant House is a masterclass in creative world design. The concept of being a tiny bear in an oversized house could have been a simple gimmick, but the developers filled every room with meaningful challenges, cleverly hidden secrets, and satisfying platforming sequences. The room-by-room structure also makes it one of the most approachable worlds for completionists since you can methodically clear one area before moving to the next.

If you enjoyed Giant House, the next world to tackle in the main progression is typically The Hive. Our [Hive walkthrough](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/) covers everything you need for that world. And for a complete overview of every mission in the game across all worlds, our [all missions guide](/blog/all-missions-guide/) provides the full roadmap from start to finish.

Introduction to Giant House

Giant House is one of the most creative and visually striking worlds in Super Bear Adventure. The entire concept revolves around a single brilliant idea: you are a tiny bear exploring an ordinary house where everything is scaled to enormous proportions. Kitchen tables tower above you like skyscrapers. Pencils on desks become balance beams. A simple staircase becomes a mountainous climb. Every familiar household object is transformed into a platforming challenge or environmental puzzle.

This world tests your platforming skills more than almost any other area in the game. The oversized environment means that falls are more punishing, jumps require more precision, and navigation demands careful planning. But the rewards for thorough exploration are substantial, with some of the rarest stickers and most generous coin deposits hidden in the most unexpected places.

This guide provides a complete room-by-room walkthrough of Giant House, covering every mission objective, secret area, puzzle solution, collectible, and boss strategy. Whether you are visiting Giant House for the first time or returning to find the last few secrets you missed, this walkthrough has everything you need. If you are still getting comfortable with the game’s controls and mechanics, our beginner guide is a great place to start before taking on this challenging world.

How to Access Giant House

Giant House becomes available after you have progressed far enough in the main game. Here is exactly what you need to do to unlock it:

  1. Complete prior world requirements. Giant House typically requires completion of at least two other worlds’ main mission lines. If you have finished Turtletown and another world like Beemothep Desert, you should be eligible. Our Turtletown walkthrough and Beemothep Desert puzzle guide can help you finish those worlds.
  2. Gather sufficient stickers. You will need approximately 25 to 30 stickers to open the Giant House portal. Check our all secret stickers locations guide if you need to boost your sticker count.
  3. Locate the portal in the hub world. The Giant House portal is designed to look like a tiny mouse hole at the base of a large wall in the hub area. It is easy to walk right past because of its small size. Look along the northern wall of the hub near the fountain.
  4. Enter the mouse hole. Walk into the mouse hole and your bear will shrink down, transitioning you into the Giant House world. You spawn inside a crack in the baseboard of the house’s main hallway.

Understanding the Giant House Mechanics

Giant House introduces several unique gameplay mechanics that set it apart from every other world in Super Bear Adventure.

Scale and Perspective

Everything in the house is roughly 20 times your size. This means that objects you would normally walk past in real life become major obstacles or platforms. A coffee mug is the size of a small building. A book lying on a table forms a ramp. A pencil becomes a tightrope. Understanding this scale is critical for navigating the environment because the game uses realistic proportions. If something looks climbable in real life, it probably is in the game.

Vertical Navigation

Giant House is the most vertical world in the game. You will spend a significant amount of time climbing upward, whether it is scaling table legs, bouncing off cushions, or riding moving objects like ceiling fans or toy trains. Falls from great heights deal substantial damage, so move carefully and look for checkpoints frequently.

Surface Physics

Different surfaces in the house have different physics properties:

  • Wood surfaces (tables, shelves, floors): Normal traction and movement speed.
  • Tile surfaces (kitchen, bathroom): Slightly slippery, especially when wet.
  • Fabric surfaces (cushions, carpets, towels): Bouncy. Jumping on fabric gives you extra height.
  • Metal surfaces (pots, pans, appliances): Normal traction but often hot in the kitchen, causing damage on contact with surfaces near the stove.
  • Glass surfaces (windows, mirrors, jars): Very slippery. Minimal traction and easy to slide off.

Household Hazards

The house is filled with environmental hazards that reflect the dangers a tiny creature would face:

  • Running water from faucets acts as a powerful current that pushes you.
  • Hot surfaces near the stove and oven deal damage on contact.
  • Electrical outlets spark periodically and create small shock zones.
  • Mousetraps are instant-kill hazards scattered in corners and along baseboards.
  • The house cat patrols certain rooms and acts as a roaming enemy.

Room-by-Room Walkthrough

Giant House contains seven main rooms plus several connecting areas. Each room has its own set of missions, secrets, and challenges. Below is a complete walkthrough of every room in the recommended exploration order.

The Main Hallway (Starting Area)

You enter Giant House through a crack in the baseboard of the main hallway. This area serves as the hub for the world, with doorways leading to each room.

Missions in this area:

  • Collect the three lost buttons scattered along the hallway floor.
  • Reach the top of the coat rack near the front door.
  • Find the hidden key under the doormat (used to access the basement later).

Secrets:

  • A secret sticker is hidden inside one of the shoes on the shoe rack. Jump inside the left boot and drop down to find it.
  • Coins are lined along the top of the baseboard molding. Wall jump up to reach them.
  • A small hole behind the umbrella stand leads to a shortcut passage between the hallway and the kitchen.

Tips: The hallway is relatively safe and serves as a good place to practice the unique movement mechanics before entering more dangerous rooms. Take time to get comfortable with the scale and jumping distances here.

The Kitchen

The kitchen is the largest and most complex room in Giant House. It is also one of the most dangerous due to hot surfaces and water hazards.

Missions in this area:

  • Cross the kitchen counter from the toaster to the sink without touching the floor.
  • Retrieve the golden spoon from inside the utensil drawer.
  • Shut off the leaking faucet by turning the oversized handle.
  • Defeat the cockroach miniboss that lives under the refrigerator.

Navigation Guide:

  1. Enter the kitchen through the doorway from the hallway. You start at floor level.
  2. Climb the first chair by jumping up the cross-support bars on the chair leg. This takes about four precise jumps.
  3. From the chair seat, jump to the kitchen table. Use the napkin holder as a stepping stone.
  4. From the table, you can reach the counter by jumping to the hanging dish towel and climbing up.
  5. On the counter, navigate past the toaster (it periodically pops up toast that can launch you), the knife block (avoid the sharp edges), and the fruit bowl (you can climb the fruit for height).
  6. Reach the sink area. The dripping faucet creates water puddles that push you. Time your movements between drips.
  7. The utensil drawer can be opened by pushing a button on the counter near the drawer. Slide down into the drawer to collect the golden spoon.

Secrets:

  • Inside the sugar jar on the counter (jump in through the open lid) is a secret sticker.
  • The microwave can be entered if you hit the button on the front. Inside are coins and a collectible item.
  • Under the refrigerator is a hidden area accessible from a gap at the back. This is also where the cockroach miniboss spawns.

Tips: The stove burners cycle on and off on a timer. Watch the pattern for about ten seconds before trying to cross the stovetop area. The burners stay off for roughly five seconds at a time, which is enough to dash across if you move quickly.

The Living Room

The living room is the most platforming-intensive room, featuring tall bookshelves, a massive sofa, and a TV entertainment center that serves as the room’s main vertical challenge.

Missions in this area:

  • Climb to the top of the bookshelf and collect the photo frame.
  • Ride the ceiling fan for a full rotation without falling.
  • Find all five remote control batteries scattered around the room.
  • Activate the TV by pressing the power button on the entertainment center.

Navigation Guide:

  1. Enter from the hallway. The living room floor is covered in carpet, giving you bouncy movement.
  2. The sofa is the central landmark. Jump onto the cushions for extra height. The bounce from the cushions can launch you to the coffee table or to the sofa arm.
  3. From the sofa arm, jump to the side table and then to the lamp. The lampshade acts as a slide that deposits you on the bookshelf.
  4. The bookshelf has multiple levels you can climb by jumping between books that stick out at different depths.
  5. At the top of the bookshelf, you can reach the ceiling fan by jumping to the pull chain and swinging to the fan blade.
  6. The entertainment center against the far wall has shelves you can climb. The TV power button is on the bottom edge of the TV, accessible from the entertainment center’s middle shelf.

Secrets:

  • Behind the sofa cushions. Push the middle cushion by attacking it three times, and it slides to reveal a gap. Drop in for a secret sticker and a coin cache.
  • Inside the potted plant next to the bookshelf. Climb the plant and drop into the pot’s soil for a hidden collectible.
  • On top of the ceiling fan. After riding a full rotation, jump to the central hub of the fan where a treasure chest sits.

Tips: The ceiling fan is one of the trickiest platforming challenges in the entire game. The blades move at a moderate speed, and you need to maintain your balance while they rotate. Stay near the wide end of the blade for the most stable footing. If you fall, you land on the sofa cushions below and can try again without taking fall damage.

The Bedroom

The bedroom features a massive bed as its centerpiece, along with a desk, wardrobe, and toy chest that all serve as platforming structures.

Missions in this area:

  • Collect the lost teddy bear’s button eye from the desk.
  • Open the music box on the nightstand.
  • Explore under the bed and clear out the dust bunny enemies.
  • Reach the top of the wardrobe.

Navigation Guide:

  1. Enter from the hallway. The bedroom has hardwood floors, so traction is normal.
  2. The bed is climbable via the dangling bedsheet on the side. Jump to the sheet and climb up like a rope.
  3. From the bed, you can jump to the nightstand and then to the desk.
  4. The desk has stacked books, a lamp, and scattered stationery that form a platforming path to the upper desk shelves.
  5. The wardrobe is the tallest structure in the room. Reach it by jumping from the desk to a hanging picture frame, then swinging to the wardrobe’s decorative molding and climbing up.

Secrets:

  • Under the bed is an entire hidden area with dust bunny enemies and a secret sticker in the far corner behind a lost sock.
  • The toy chest can be opened by ground-pounding its latch. Inside are coins and a unique toy soldier cosmetic item.
  • The music box, when activated, plays a melody that opens a secret panel on the wall behind the nightstand. Inside the panel is a treasure chest.

Tips: The dust bunnies under the bed are weak individually but attack in groups. Use area attacks or spin moves to handle multiple enemies at once. The area under the bed is dark, so watch for their glowing eyes to track their positions.

The Bathroom

The bathroom is the most hazard-dense room due to water everywhere. It features a bathtub, toilet, and sink that each present unique challenges.

Missions in this area:

  • Navigate across the flooded floor to reach the medicine cabinet.
  • Collect the rubber duck from the bathtub.
  • Drain the overflowing sink by removing the stopper.
  • Find the hidden pipe passage to the basement.

Navigation Guide:

  1. The bathroom floor is partially flooded with shallow water. Movement through water is slow, so use the bath mat and scattered items as dry stepping stones.
  2. The toilet is climbable via the towel hanging from a rack nearby. Use it as a swing to reach the toilet seat, then jump to the tank top.
  3. From the toilet tank, jump to the sink counter. The soap dish and toothbrush holder serve as platforms.
  4. The medicine cabinet above the sink can be reached by climbing the mirror frame. Inside the cabinet, shelves hold collectibles and mission items.
  5. The bathtub is accessible via the bath mat. The rubber duck floats in the water and acts as a moving platform.

Secrets:

  • Inside the drain of the bathtub (when you manage to pull the plug) is a pipe passage leading to a hidden underground room with a treasure chest.
  • Behind the toilet is a secret sticker wedged between the toilet and the wall.
  • The medicine cabinet’s top shelf has a secret compartment that opens when you push a specific bottle to the left.

Tips: The flooded floor creates a current that pushes you toward the drain. Stay on the bath mat and dry objects when possible. If you do get swept into the current, jump repeatedly to slow your drift and work your way to the nearest solid ground.

The Attic

The attic is an optional area accessed by climbing through a hatch in the bedroom ceiling. It is one of the most rewarding areas in Giant House.

Missions in this area:

  • Find the three pieces of the torn photograph.
  • Activate the old radio.
  • Clear out the spider web barriers blocking the back of the attic.

Navigation Guide:

  1. Access the attic from the top of the bedroom wardrobe. A ceiling hatch is within jumping distance from the wardrobe’s top.
  2. The attic is filled with cardboard boxes, old furniture, and holiday decorations. These form natural platforms and pathways.
  3. Spider webs block several areas. Attack them to clear them, but be prepared for spider enemies to drop from above.
  4. The old radio is on a high shelf at the back of the attic. Reach it by climbing stacked boxes.

Secrets:

  • A treasure chest sits inside a hollowed-out globe on the shelf near the window.
  • A secret sticker is taped to the underside of a cardboard box flap. You need to jump under the flap and look up to spot it.
  • Christmas ornaments hanging from the rafters contain coins when broken.

Tips: The attic spiders are the strongest basic enemies in Giant House. They can web-shoot to slow you down and they drop from the ceiling without warning. Listen for the subtle clicking sound that precedes their ambush attacks.

The Basement

The basement is the final area of Giant House and the location of the world boss. It is dark, damp, and filled with the toughest enemies in the world.

Missions in this area:

  • Restore power to the basement by flipping the breaker switch.
  • Navigate through the storage shelves to find the boss arena.
  • Defeat the Giant House boss.

Navigation Guide:

  1. Access the basement either through the hidden pipe passage in the bathroom or by using the key found under the hallway doormat to unlock the basement door.
  2. The basement is initially dark. Navigate carefully using the faint light from small windows until you reach the breaker panel.
  3. After restoring power, the basement lights up, revealing storage shelves, a workbench, and a furnace. The boss arena is in the cleared area behind the furnace.

Secrets:

  • The workbench has a vice grip holding a treasure chest. Interact with the vice handle to release it.
  • Behind the water heater is a secret sticker and a pile of coins.
  • A loose brick in the basement wall near the stairs can be pushed to reveal a small nook with a rare collectible.

Do I need to complete Giant House to access later worlds?

Giant House completion contributes to your overall sticker count and mission progress, which may be required for accessing later worlds. However, the specific requirements vary. Check our all missions guide for the full progression path.

The game runs slowly in Giant House. How can I fix it?

Giant House has more complex geometry than most worlds due to the detailed room environments. If you experience lag, our lag fix and optimization guide has specific settings recommendations. Lowering shadow quality and reducing particle effects will have the biggest impact in this world.

I cannot find the basement key. Where is it?

The basement key is under the doormat at the front door in the main hallway. You need to ground-pound the doormat to reveal it. It is one of the first things available in the world, but many players overlook it.

Is there a way to fast travel between rooms?

There is no formal fast travel system within Giant House. However, the shortcut passage behind the umbrella stand in the hallway connects directly to the kitchen, and the bathroom pipe passage connects to the basement. These shortcuts save significant travel time once discovered.

Final Thoughts on Giant House

Giant House is a masterclass in creative world design. The concept of being a tiny bear in an oversized house could have been a simple gimmick, but the developers filled every room with meaningful challenges, cleverly hidden secrets, and satisfying platforming sequences. The room-by-room structure also makes it one of the most approachable worlds for completionists since you can methodically clear one area before moving to the next.

If you enjoyed Giant House, the next world to tackle in the main progression is typically The Hive. Our Hive walkthrough covers everything you need for that world. And for a complete overview of every mission in the game across all worlds, our all missions guide provides the full roadmap from start to finish.

How to Get All Medals in Super Bear Adventure - 100% Completion Guide
Complete guide to earning every medal in Super Bear Adventure. Step-by-step walkthroughs for Bronze, Silver, and Gold medals in all worlds.

## Understanding the Medal System in Super Bear Adventure

![Bronze medal reward](/images/items/medal-bronze.svg)

Medals are one of the core progression systems in Super Bear Adventure, rewarding players for accomplishing specific challenges in each world. Whether you are a casual player looking to earn a few extra rewards or a completionist aiming for 100% game completion, understanding how medals work is essential.

The medal system was introduced in version 6.0.0 and has been expanded with each subsequent [game update](/blog/version-changelogs/). Today, there are dozens of medals to earn across all worlds, each testing a different skill or encouraging thorough exploration.

If you are just getting started with Super Bear Adventure, we recommend reading our [beginner guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) first to make sure you have a solid grasp of the game's basic mechanics before pursuing medals.

## Medal Types and Tiers

### Medal Tiers

Each challenge in Super Bear Adventure awards medals in three tiers:

#### Bronze Medal

- The easiest tier to achieve
- Typically requires completing the basic objective
- Generous time limits for timed challenges
- Good starting point for casual players
- Rewards: Small coin bonus and bronze badge

#### Silver Medal

![Silver medal reward](/images/items/medal-silver.svg)

- Moderate difficulty
- Requires better performance than bronze
- Tighter time limits or higher completion thresholds
- Rewards: Medium coin bonus, silver badge, and sometimes cosmetic items

#### Gold Medal

![Gold medal reward](/images/items/medal-gold.svg)

- The highest tier and most difficult to achieve
- Requires near-perfect performance
- Very tight time limits or 100% completion requirements
- Rewards: Large coin bonus, gold badge, and exclusive cosmetics or [skins](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/)

### Medal Categories

Medals fall into several categories based on the type of challenge:

#### Completion Medals

Awarded for finding all collectibles in a specific area. This includes coins, [stickers](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/), hidden items, and other world-specific collectibles. These medals encourage thorough exploration and reward players who check every corner.

#### Time Trial Medals

Awarded for completing specific routes or missions within time limits. Each tier has a different time threshold, with gold requiring exceptional speed and route knowledge. These medals reward players who have mastered the game's movement mechanics.

#### Combat Medals

Awarded for defeating enemies or [bosses](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) under specific conditions. This might include defeating a boss without taking damage, defeating a certain number of enemies within a time limit, or using specific attack techniques.

#### Exploration Medals

Awarded for discovering [secret areas](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/), reaching specific vantage points, or finding hidden paths. These medals celebrate curiosity and thoroughness.

#### Mission Medals

Tied to the [game's missions](/blog/all-missions-guide/) and awarded for completing mission objectives with various levels of success. Mission medals often have optional objectives that count toward higher tiers.

## Turtletown Medals

Turtletown is the first world and has the most accessible medals, making it an excellent place to learn medal-hunting strategies. For complete world coverage, see our [Turtletown walkthrough](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/).

### Completion Medals

#### Coin Collector

- **Bronze**: Collect 50% of all coins in Turtletown
- **Silver**: Collect 75% of all coins in Turtletown
- **Gold**: Collect 100% of all coins in Turtletown
- **Tips**: Coins respawn when you re-enter the world, but the medal tracks unique coin locations found. Check underwater areas and behind waterfalls for hidden coin clusters.

#### Shell Seeker

- **Bronze**: Find 5 hidden turtle shells
- **Silver**: Find 10 hidden turtle shells
- **Gold**: Find all 15 hidden turtle shells
- **Tips**: Turtle shells are placed in non-obvious locations. Check behind large rocks, inside hollow trees, and on elevated platforms that require precise jumping to reach.

#### Sticker Album - Turtletown

- **Bronze**: Find 3 stickers in Turtletown
- **Silver**: Find 6 stickers in Turtletown
- **Gold**: Find all 10 stickers in Turtletown
- **Tips**: Stickers are among the hardest collectibles to find. Use our [sticker locations guide](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/) for exact positions.

### Time Trial Medals

#### Beach Sprint

- **Bronze**: Complete the beach course in under 3:00
- **Silver**: Complete the beach course in under 2:00
- **Gold**: Complete the beach course in under 1:15
- **Tips**: Use double jumps to cut corners and skip platforming sections. The optimal route hugs the left side of the beach at the start, then cuts through the cave shortcut.

#### Lighthouse Climb

- **Bronze**: Reach the top of the lighthouse in under 2:30
- **Silver**: Reach the top of the lighthouse in under 1:45
- **Gold**: Reach the top of the lighthouse in under 1:00
- **Tips**: Wall jumps are essential for gold. Instead of using the spiral path, chain wall jumps on the interior walls to skip multiple floors.

### Combat Medals

#### Crab Crusher

- **Bronze**: Defeat 10 crabs without taking damage
- **Silver**: Defeat 20 crabs without taking damage
- **Gold**: Defeat 30 crabs without taking damage
- **Tips**: Use ground pound attacks to defeat crabs safely since they cannot hit you during the ground pound animation. Chain attacks between groups for efficient clearing.

#### Guardian's Challenge

- **Bronze**: Defeat the Turtletown boss
- **Silver**: Defeat the boss taking fewer than 5 hits
- **Gold**: Defeat the boss without taking any damage
- **Tips**: Learn the boss's three-phase pattern thoroughly. Phase transitions always begin with the same tell animation, giving you time to reposition.

## Snow Valley Medals

Snow Valley introduces ice physics that make many medals more challenging. Our [Snow Valley secrets guide](/blog/snow-valley-secrets/) covers the world in detail.

### Completion Medals

#### Frozen Treasure Hunter

- **Bronze**: Collect 50% of Snow Valley's coins
- **Silver**: Collect 75% of Snow Valley's coins
- **Gold**: Collect 100% of Snow Valley's coins
- **Tips**: Many coins in Snow Valley are hidden inside breakable ice blocks. Use ground pound near suspicious ice formations to reveal hidden coins. The ice caves contain the highest concentration of easy-to-miss coins.

#### Snowflake Collection

- **Bronze**: Find 5 unique snowflake crystals
- **Silver**: Find 10 unique snowflake crystals
- **Gold**: Find all 15 unique snowflake crystals
- **Tips**: Snowflake crystals glow faintly, making them visible in dark areas. During blizzard conditions, their glow becomes more apparent. Check mountain ledges and inside igloos.

### Time Trial Medals

#### Ice Sliding Sprint

- **Bronze**: Complete the ice slide course in under 2:30
- **Silver**: Complete the ice slide course in under 1:45
- **Gold**: Complete the ice slide course in under 1:10
- **Tips**: Master the ice physics. On ice surfaces, start your turns early because momentum carries you further than on normal terrain. The gold medal route requires using a hidden ramp shortcut halfway through the course.

#### Mountain Peak Rush

- **Bronze**: Reach the summit in under 4:00
- **Silver**: Reach the summit in under 2:45
- **Gold**: Reach the summit in under 1:50
- **Tips**: This is one of the hardest time trials in the game. The gold medal requires knowledge of wall jump shortcuts and specific rock formations that serve as ledge grabs. Practice the route in free exploration before attempting timed runs.

### Combat Medals

#### Wolf Pack Survivor

- **Bronze**: Survive 3 waves of frost wolves
- **Silver**: Survive 5 waves without healing
- **Gold**: Survive all 7 waves without taking damage
- **Tips**: Position yourself with a wall behind you so wolves cannot attack from all directions. The alpha wolf always appears in waves 4 and 7. Prioritize it to cause the pack to scatter.

## Beemothep Desert Medals

The desert world combines exploration challenges with puzzle-solving medals. Full walkthrough in our [Beemothep Desert guide](/blog/beemothep-desert-puzzle/).

### Completion Medals

#### Tomb Raider

- **Bronze**: Discover 3 hidden tomb chambers
- **Silver**: Discover 6 hidden tomb chambers
- **Gold**: Discover all 10 hidden tomb chambers
- **Tips**: Tomb chambers are hidden behind false walls and buried sand mounds. Look for subtle visual cues like slightly different colored stone or sand that seems disturbed. Some chambers require solving environmental puzzles to access.

#### Desert Artifact Collector

- **Bronze**: Find 5 ancient artifacts
- **Silver**: Find 10 ancient artifacts
- **Gold**: Find all 20 ancient artifacts
- **Tips**: Artifacts are scattered across the desert, many in hard-to-reach locations. The Archaeologist Lizard NPC provides hints about artifact locations if you complete his side quests.

### Puzzle Medals

#### Hieroglyphic Decoder

- **Bronze**: Solve 3 hieroglyphic puzzles
- **Silver**: Solve 7 hieroglyphic puzzles
- **Gold**: Solve all 12 hieroglyphic puzzles
- **Tips**: Each hieroglyphic puzzle follows a logic pattern. Look for matching symbols, sequential numbers, or mirror patterns. The puzzles get progressively harder, with later ones requiring information gathered from earlier puzzles.

#### Pyramid Navigator

- **Bronze**: Navigate the pyramid's first floor
- **Silver**: Navigate to the pyramid's third floor
- **Gold**: Navigate to the pyramid's summit
- **Tips**: The pyramid is a massive maze with traps. Memorize trap patterns rather than trying to react to them. Some traps have safe spots where you can pause and observe the next section's timing.

## Giant House Medals

The Giant House world uses scale-based challenges for its medals. See our [Giant House walkthrough](/blog/giant-house-walkthrough/).

### Completion Medals

#### Tiny Explorer

- **Bronze**: Explore 50% of the Giant House
- **Silver**: Explore 75% of the Giant House
- **Gold**: Explore 100% of the Giant House
- **Tips**: The house has rooms that are easy to miss because entrances are hidden at unusual scales. Check behind giant furniture, inside open drawers, and through cracks in walls.

### Platforming Medals

#### Kitchen Climber

- **Bronze**: Reach the top of the kitchen counter
- **Silver**: Reach the top of the kitchen counter in under 2:00
- **Gold**: Reach the top via the speed route in under 1:00
- **Tips**: Use utensils, handles, and towels as platforms. The gold medal route uses a series of precise jumps from the toaster to the knife rack to the spice shelf.

#### Bookshelf Ascent

- **Bronze**: Climb to the top shelf
- **Silver**: Climb to the top shelf collecting all coins
- **Gold**: Climb to the top shelf in under 1:30 collecting all coins
- **Tips**: Books can be pushed to create new platforms. Plan your route before starting the timed attempt to know which books need moving and in which order.

## The Hive Medals

The Hive's medals focus on navigation and combat in its honeycomb structure. Details in our [Hive walkthrough](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/).

### Navigation Medals

#### Honeycomb Navigator

- **Bronze**: Navigate through 5 honeycomb chambers
- **Silver**: Navigate through 10 honeycomb chambers
- **Gold**: Navigate through all 18 honeycomb chambers
- **Tips**: The honeycomb structure can be disorienting. Use the hexagonal patterns to maintain your sense of direction. Each chamber has six possible exits, but only some are real paths.

### Combat Medals

#### Royal Guard Challenge

- **Bronze**: Defeat the Royal Guard boss
- **Silver**: Defeat the Royal Guard in under 5:00
- **Gold**: Defeat the Royal Guard without taking damage in under 3:00
- **Tips**: The Royal Guard has three phases. In phase one, dodge lance thrusts and counterattack. Phase two adds aerial attacks that require precise dodging. Phase three combines both with drone reinforcements that you must manage simultaneously.

## Mole Mines Medals

The newest world's medals focus on mining and underground exploration. See our [Mole Mines walkthrough](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/).

### Mining Medals

#### Gem Collector

- **Bronze**: Collect 10 unique gems
- **Silver**: Collect 25 unique gems
- **Gold**: Collect all 50 unique gems
- **Tips**: Gems are embedded in mine walls and require specific mining actions to extract. Look for sparkling spots on walls and use ground pound near them to reveal gems.

#### Mine Cart Master

- **Bronze**: Complete a mine cart run
- **Silver**: Complete a mine cart run collecting 80% of track coins
- **Gold**: Complete a perfect mine cart run with all coins and no crashes
- **Tips**: Mine cart sequences are fast-paced. Memorize the track layout including jump points, lean directions, and track switches. The gold medal has zero tolerance for missed coins or track collisions.

## General Tips for Medal Hunting

![Map overview for exploration planning](/images/gameplay/map-overview.svg)

### Prioritize Exploration First

Before attempting any timed or combat medals, thoroughly explore each world in free play mode. Knowing the layout, enemy positions, and hidden paths is essential for achieving gold medals. This exploration will also help you discover [secret areas and easter eggs](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/) that might contain medal-related content.

### Practice Movement Mechanics

Many gold medals require advanced movement techniques:

- **Chain jumping**: Jumping immediately upon landing preserves momentum
- **Wall jump cancels**: Canceling a wall jump with a directional input can reach otherwise inaccessible areas
- **Ground pound bouncing**: Ground pounding onto certain surfaces bounces Baaren higher than a normal jump
- **Slide jumping**: Jumping while sliding on slopes gives extra speed and distance

### Use the Right Equipment

Some [skins and hats](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/) provide subtle advantages that can help with medals. While the differences are mostly cosmetic, certain items may affect animation timing in ways that experienced players can exploit.

### Multiplayer Medal Strategies

Playing in [multiplayer](/blog/multiplayer-guide/) can help with certain medal challenges. While medals are earned individually, having teammates can:

- Help you identify collectible locations you might have missed
- Provide distraction for enemies during combat medals
- Offer tips and strategies in real time
- Make grinding for coin-collection medals less tedious

### Track Your Progress

Use the in-game medal tracker to see which medals you still need and what is required for each tier. This prevents wasted effort on medals you have already earned and helps you plan efficient medal-hunting sessions.

### Coin Farming for Medal-Related Purchases

Some medal challenges become easier with purchased upgrades or items. If you need coins to prepare for medal challenges, check our [coin farming guide](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/) for efficient methods. Alternatively, the [mod APK with unlimited money](/blog/super-bear-adventure-unlimited-money/) removes the coin barrier entirely.

## Rewards for Medal Completion

### Individual Medal Rewards

Each medal awards:
- **Bronze**: 50-100 coins
- **Silver**: 150-300 coins
- **Gold**: 500-1000 coins

Some gold medals also award exclusive cosmetic items that cannot be obtained any other way.

### World Completion Rewards

Earning all gold medals in a single world awards:
- A world-specific exclusive skin
- A large coin bonus (5000+)
- A special badge displayed on your profile
- Access to a hidden area within that world

### Full Completion Reward

Earning every gold medal in the game awards:
- The legendary "Medal Master" skin
- 25,000 coins
- An exclusive animated profile badge
- Access to a secret bonus area

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Do medals carry over between game versions?

Yes, your medal progress is saved to your account and carries over through [game updates](/blog/version-changelogs/). New updates may add additional medals but never remove existing ones.

### Can I earn medals in the mod version?

Using the [mod APK](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-apk-download/) does not prevent medal earning. However, using certain [mod menu](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-menu/) features like invincibility may disqualify specific combat medals that require no-damage completion.

### What are the hardest medals to earn?

The community generally considers these the most difficult gold medals:
1. Mountain Peak Rush (Snow Valley) - extremely tight time requirement
2. Royal Guard No-Damage (The Hive) - demanding combat perfection
3. Perfect Mine Cart Run (Mole Mines) - requires memorization and reflexes
4. Pyramid Summit (Beemothep Desert) - complex navigation under pressure

### Can I reset my medal progress?

Medal progress cannot be selectively reset. If you want to re-earn medals, you would need to start a new save file. This is generally not recommended since medal progress represents significant time investment.

## Conclusion

Earning all medals in Super Bear Adventure is one of the game's most satisfying accomplishments. The journey from your first bronze medal to the legendary Medal Master skin represents a true mastery of everything the game has to offer. Take it one world at a time, celebrate each tier you achieve, and do not get discouraged by the more challenging gold medals. With practice and the strategies outlined in this guide, 100% medal completion is absolutely achievable.

Playing in multiplayer can help with certain medal challenges. While medals are earned individually, having teammates can:

  • Help you identify collectible locations you might have missed
  • Provide distraction for enemies during combat medals
  • Offer tips and strategies in real time
  • Make grinding for coin-collection medals less tedious

Track Your Progress

Use the in-game medal tracker to see which medals you still need and what is required for each tier. This prevents wasted effort on medals you have already earned and helps you plan efficient medal-hunting sessions.

Coin Farming for Medal-Related Purchases

Some medal challenges become easier with purchased upgrades or items. If you need coins to prepare for medal challenges, check our coin farming guide for efficient methods. Alternatively, the mod APK with unlimited money removes the coin barrier entirely.

Rewards for Medal Completion

Individual Medal Rewards

Each medal awards:

  • Bronze: 50-100 coins
  • Silver: 150-300 coins
  • Gold: 500-1000 coins

Some gold medals also award exclusive cosmetic items that cannot be obtained any other way.

World Completion Rewards

Earning all gold medals in a single world awards:

  • A world-specific exclusive skin
  • A large coin bonus (5000+)
  • A special badge displayed on your profile
  • Access to a hidden area within that world

Full Completion Reward

Earning every gold medal in the game awards:

  • The legendary “Medal Master” skin
  • 25,000 coins
  • An exclusive animated profile badge
  • Access to a secret bonus area

Frequently Asked Questions

Do medals carry over between game versions?

Yes, your medal progress is saved to your account and carries over through game updates. New updates may add additional medals but never remove existing ones.

Can I earn medals in the mod version?

Using the mod APK does not prevent medal earning. However, using certain mod menu features like invincibility may disqualify specific combat medals that require no-damage completion.

What are the hardest medals to earn?

The community generally considers these the most difficult gold medals:

  1. Mountain Peak Rush (Snow Valley) - extremely tight time requirement
  2. Royal Guard No-Damage (The Hive) - demanding combat perfection
  3. Perfect Mine Cart Run (Mole Mines) - requires memorization and reflexes
  4. Pyramid Summit (Beemothep Desert) - complex navigation under pressure

Can I reset my medal progress?

Medal progress cannot be selectively reset. If you want to re-earn medals, you would need to start a new save file. This is generally not recommended since medal progress represents significant time investment.

Conclusion

Earning all medals in Super Bear Adventure is one of the game’s most satisfying accomplishments. The journey from your first bronze medal to the legendary Medal Master skin represents a true mastery of everything the game has to offer. Take it one world at a time, celebrate each tier you achieve, and do not get discouraged by the more challenging gold medals. With practice and the strategies outlined in this guide, 100% medal completion is absolutely achievable.

Mole Mines Walkthrough: Mining Carts, Gem Puzzles & All Secrets in Super Bear Adventure
Complete Mole Mines walkthrough for Super Bear Adventure with mining cart sections, gem puzzle solutions, secret areas, and boss fight tips.
How to Complete Mole Mines in Super Bear Adventure
Complete all missions and find all secrets in Mole Mines
Enter the mines and board the mine cart
Start at the mine entrance. Board the mine cart at the station. Jump at split track points to choose your path.
In the crystal cavern section, match colored gems to the floor patterns. Push crystals into the correct positions.
Use the stepping stones and avoid the underground fish enemies. The lake section has stickers on the far shore.
Complete all Mole Council missions in the lower mine area. These involve collecting specific crystals and defeating mole guards.
Attack during the crystal-throw animation. The Mole King is vulnerable when his back is turned during his throwing sequence.

## Welcome to the Mole Mines

The Mole Mines is one of the most thrilling worlds in Super Bear Adventure, taking players deep underground into a sprawling network of tunnels, caverns, and crystal-filled chambers. Unlike the open-air environments of [Turtletown](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/) or [Snow Valley](/blog/snow-valley-secrets/), the Mole Mines feel claustrophobic and intense, with tight corridors, darkness mechanics, and high-speed mining cart sections that test your reflexes.

This world introduces two major gameplay systems: mining cart rides where you must dodge obstacles and switch tracks at high speed, and gem puzzles that require you to collect and place colored gems in specific patterns to unlock new areas. Both mechanics create a gameplay loop that is distinct from anything else in Super Bear Adventure.

If you are new to the game, we strongly recommend completing our [beginner guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) first. The Mole Mines is considered an advanced world, and understanding core mechanics like combat, jumping, and item management will help you avoid frustration in the more demanding sections.

## How to Access the Mole Mines

![Mole Mines entrance](/images/worlds/detail/mines-entrance.svg)

The Mole Mines entrance is located on the northern edge of the overworld hub, behind a large boulder. To move the boulder, you need to have completed at least four main missions. Once the boulder is cleared, a mine shaft elevator becomes accessible. Ride it down to enter the Mole Mines.

Before descending, stock up on supplies. The Mole Mines has fewer health pickups than most other worlds, so purchasing extra health items from the shop is wise. If you need coins for supplies, our [coin farming guide](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/) has efficient strategies for building your funds quickly.

## Mining Cart Mechanics Explained

![Mining cart ride through the tunnels](/images/worlds/detail/mines-cart-ride.svg)

Mining cart sections are the signature feature of the Mole Mines. These are on-rails segments where your bear rides in a mine cart through twisting tunnels at high speed. Understanding how these sections work is essential for survival and for finding secrets.

### Basic Controls

During mining cart sections, your bear automatically moves forward on the track. You have three actions available. First, you can lean left or right to shift the cart between parallel tracks. This is essential for avoiding obstacles like fallen rocks and broken track sections. Second, you can jump by pressing the jump button, which makes the cart briefly leave the track to hop over gaps and low barriers. Third, you can duck by pressing the crouch button, which lowers your bear's profile to pass under low-hanging stalactites and wooden beams.

### Track Switching

At certain points during cart rides, the track splits into two or three parallel paths. A visual indicator appears on screen showing which tracks are available. Leaning left or right switches between tracks. The key detail many players miss is that track switches are not instant. There is about a half-second transition period where your cart moves laterally. During this transition, you are vulnerable to obstacles on both the origin and destination tracks. Plan your switches early when you see an obstacle ahead rather than waiting until the last moment.

### Speed Boosts and Brakes

Some track sections have golden rail segments that boost your speed. These are fun but also make reaction windows shorter for upcoming obstacles. Red rail segments slow you down. While slower sections might feel like a reprieve, they often precede the most obstacle-dense portions of the track, so stay alert.

### Collectibles on Tracks

Coins and gems are scattered along the tracks during cart rides. They usually appear in lines that indicate the safe track to follow. If you see a line of coins on the left track, it is generally safe to stay on that track until the coins end. However, some advanced sections deliberately place coins on dangerous tracks to tempt greedy players. Trust obstacle cues over coin placement when they conflict.

## Gem Puzzle System

![Gem puzzle mechanics in the Mole Mines](/images/worlds/detail/mines-gem-puzzle.svg)

The Mole Mines features a color-coded gem puzzle system that gates progression through the world. There are four gem colors: red, blue, green, and yellow. Each gem type is found in specific areas of the mines, and you need to collect gems and place them in matching pedestals to unlock doors and activate mechanisms.

### Finding Gems

Gems are not just lying on the ground. Most are earned by completing challenges within the Mole Mines. Red gems come from defeating mini-boss mole enemies in combat arenas. Blue gems are rewards for completing mining cart sections without taking damage. Green gems are hidden in secret areas behind breakable walls. Yellow gems are obtained by solving environmental puzzles like pressure plate sequences and lever combinations.

### Gem Pedestals

Throughout the Mole Mines, you will find hexagonal pedestals with colored gem-shaped slots. Walking up to a pedestal with the matching gem in your inventory automatically places the gem. Each pedestal is part of a larger puzzle -- some doors require one gem, while the final door before the boss requires all four colors.

### The Master Gem Gate

The most complex puzzle in the Mole Mines is the Master Gem Gate, located in Area 5. This gate requires you to place gems in a specific sequence. The correct order is revealed by examining the murals on the walls of the surrounding cavern. The murals depict the four gem colors in order from top to bottom: green, red, yellow, blue. Place the gems in this order to open the gate. Placing them in the wrong order resets all four pedestals, forcing you to replace them.

## Area-by-Area Walkthrough

### Area 1: The Upper Tunnels

You begin in a series of dimly lit tunnels close to the surface. The lighting here is slightly better than deeper areas, easing you into the underground atmosphere. The Upper Tunnels are primarily a combat zone with groups of mole enemies.

Mole enemies come in two types. Digger moles pop out of the ground and attack with their claws. They are vulnerable for about two seconds after surfacing. Armored moles wear helmets and require a ground pound attack to stun them before you can deal damage with normal attacks.

The Upper Tunnels contain your first red gem. To earn it, defeat the mini-boss digger mole in the large cavern at the end of the area. This mole is larger than normal digger moles, has three times the health, and can burrow and resurface behind you. Keep moving in circles and attack during its surface windows.

A side tunnel on the left leads to a small crystal cave with a hidden sticker. For all sticker locations across every world, see our [sticker locations guide](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/).

### Area 2: The Crystal Caverns

The Crystal Caverns are a breathtaking open area filled with enormous glowing crystals in various colors. The cavern is the vertical hub of the Mole Mines, with paths branching off at different heights. You will return here multiple times as you explore deeper areas.

The main feature of the Crystal Caverns is the crystal light puzzle. Large crystals can be rotated by punching them, and when rotated to the correct angle, they refract light beams to illuminate dark passages. There are three crystals that need to be aligned to open the path to Area 3.

Crystal one should be rotated twice (two punches) to point its beam at the mirror on the west wall. Crystal two needs four punches to direct its beam to the east mirror. Crystal three needs one punch to send its beam to the ceiling reflector, which splits the light and illuminates the entire cavern, revealing the previously hidden entrance to Area 3.

While in the Crystal Caverns, explore the lower level by dropping down from the main platform. A series of small ledges leads to a hidden alcove with a green gem and 100 bonus coins.

### Area 3: The Mine Cart Express

Area 3 is the first major mining cart section. This is a long, continuous cart ride through winding tunnels with increasing difficulty.

The ride begins slowly with simple obstacles: single rocks on the track that require a lane change, and low beams that require ducking. After about 30 seconds, the speed increases and obstacles come in combinations. You might need to switch lanes and immediately duck, or jump a gap and then switch lanes before landing.

The midpoint of the ride features a dramatic section where the track runs along the edge of a massive underground chasm. The track itself is partially broken here, requiring precise jumps over three consecutive gaps. The timing is tight -- jump as soon as the front of the cart reaches the broken edge.

At the end of the cart ride, if you completed the section without taking any damage, you receive a blue gem. If you took damage, you can replay the section by walking back to the cart loading area.

There is a secret during this cart ride. About halfway through, during the chasm section, look for a side track branching to the right just before the second gap. Switch to the right track instead of jumping the gap. This side track leads to a hidden room with a sticker and a shortcut back to the Crystal Caverns.

### Area 4: The Deep Tunnels

The Deep Tunnels are the darkest area of the Mole Mines. Your visibility is limited to a small radius around your bear, lit by a helmet lamp that activates automatically. The limited visibility makes enemy encounters more tense, as mole enemies can ambush you from the darkness.

Navigation in the Deep Tunnels relies on sound cues and environmental hints. Dripping water sounds indicate a turn to the right. Rumbling sounds mean an armored mole is nearby. A faint musical chime means you are near a secret or collectible.

The yellow gem is obtained in the Deep Tunnels by solving a lever puzzle. There are five levers spread across the area, each labeled with a symbol. The correct order to pull them is: pickaxe, gem, cart, mole, bear. This order is hinted at by a sign near the entrance to the Deep Tunnels, though the sign is partially obscured and easy to miss.

After collecting the yellow gem, a new tunnel opens leading to Area 5.

### Area 5: The Mole King's Domain

Area 5 is the pre-boss area containing the Master Gem Gate. Before the gate, there is a rest area with a save point, health refill, and a friendly mole NPC who offers hints about the boss fight.

The Master Gem Gate requires all four gems placed in the correct sequence (green, red, yellow, blue, as described above). Once the gate opens, you enter the boss arena.

### Area 6: The Mole King Boss Fight

![Mole King boss arena](/images/worlds/detail/mines-boss.svg)

The Mole King is the boss of the Mole Mines. He is a massive mole wearing a crown of crystals and wielding a giant pickaxe. For strategies on every boss in the game, check our [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/).

The Mole King fight has three distinct phases.

Phase one involves the Mole King slamming his pickaxe into the ground, creating shockwaves that travel outward in straight lines. Jump over the shockwaves and attack him during the recovery animation after each slam. Three attack windows are needed to complete phase one.

Phase two has the Mole King burrowing underground and surfacing at random points in the arena, similar to the digger moles but much larger. When he surfaces, he creates a large area-of-effect tremor that stuns you if you are standing on the ground. To avoid this, stand on one of the raised rock platforms around the edge of the arena. After surfacing, he is dazed for about three seconds. Drop down and attack him during this window.

Phase three combines both previous mechanics. The Mole King stands in the center and alternates between pickaxe slams and burrowing dives. He also summons armored mole minions throughout this phase. Clear the minions when you can, but prioritize dodging the Mole King's attacks and hitting him during his vulnerability windows.

After defeating the Mole King, he drops the Crystal Crown, a key item needed for a late-game overworld mission. He also drops a large cache of coins and the Mole Mines completion badge.

## All Secrets in the Mole Mines

The Mole Mines has 10 secrets to discover. Finding them all unlocks the Miner Bear cosmetic hat. For more hidden items and easter eggs across the entire game, visit our [secret areas and easter eggs guide](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/).

1. **Crystal Cave Sticker** -- Left side tunnel in the Upper Tunnels, behind a group of digger moles.
2. **Hidden Coin Cache** -- Lower level of the Crystal Caverns, accessible by dropping down from the main platform.
3. **Green Gem Alcove** -- Same lower level area as Secret 2, in a hidden alcove behind crystal formations.
4. **Side Track Room** -- During the Mine Cart Express, take the hidden right branch before the second chasm gap.
5. **Sound Puzzle Room** -- In the Deep Tunnels, follow a specific sequence of sound cues (drip-rumble-drip-chime) to find a hidden door.
6. **Behind the Waterfall** -- An underground waterfall in the Deep Tunnels hides a cave passage behind it containing 200 coins.
7. **Crystal Cavern Ceiling** -- Use the crystal light beams as platforms (they become solid after solving the light puzzle) to reach a platform on the ceiling with a sticker.
8. **Mole NPC Secret** -- Talk to the friendly mole NPC in Area 5 three times. On the third conversation, he gives you a map fragment revealing a secret in Area 1 you likely missed.
9. **Boss Arena Pre-fight** -- Before triggering the boss, explore the edges of the arena. A breakable wall on the south side leads to a small room with the final sticker.
10. **Post-Boss Tunnel** -- After defeating the Mole King, his burrowing opens a new tunnel in the arena floor. Drop in to find a bonus room with 500 coins and a rare skin unlock. Check our [skins and hats guide](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/) for details.

## Tips for Mastering the Mole Mines

### Mining Cart Survival Tips

Memorize obstacle patterns. Each cart section has a fixed layout, so repeated runs let you learn the sequence. Focus on survival first, then optimize for coin collection on subsequent runs.

When approaching jumps over gaps, keep in mind that the cart has slight forward momentum. Jump a tiny bit earlier than you think you need to. Late jumps are the number one cause of cart section failures.

### Gem Puzzle Tips

Keep track of which gems you have collected. The game does show your gem inventory in the pause menu, but it is easy to forget which puzzles you have already solved. If you are missing a gem and cannot remember which one, the friendly mole NPC in Area 5 gives hints about gem locations.

### Combat Tips in Darkness

In the Deep Tunnels, use the ground pound attack frequently. Its area-of-effect damage can hit mole enemies you cannot see in the darkness. The shockwave from a ground pound briefly illuminates a larger area, acting as a sort of sonar.

### Performance Tips

The Mole Mines has heavy lighting and particle effects, especially in the Crystal Caverns. If you experience frame drops or lag, our [lag fix and optimization guide](/blog/lag-fix-optimization/) covers settings adjustments that can help without sacrificing too much visual quality.

## Connection to Other Worlds

The Mole Mines connects to the broader Super Bear Adventure story in several ways. The Crystal Crown from the Mole King is required for one of the final overworld missions, which ties together items from multiple worlds. This mission chain is covered in our [all missions guide](/blog/all-missions-guide/).

Some tunnels in the Mole Mines have visual connections to other worlds. One Deep Tunnel section has a glass wall looking into what appears to be the underground portion of [The Hive](/blog/the-hive-walkthrough/), suggesting the two worlds are geographically connected in the game's lore. For more on the game's story and world connections, see our [lore and story guide](/blog/lore-baaren-story/).

The Mole Mines cart mechanics also appear in a mini-game in the [Arcade World](/blog/arcade-world-mini-games/), where you race through a procedurally generated track for a high score.

## Conclusion

The Mole Mines offers one of the most varied and exciting experiences in Super Bear Adventure. Between the adrenaline of mining cart rides, the satisfaction of solving gem puzzles, and the tension of navigating dark tunnels, this world has something for every type of player. Use this walkthrough to find every secret, solve every puzzle, and defeat the Mole King with confidence.

For your next challenge, consider tackling the [Beemothep Desert](/blog/beemothep-desert-puzzle/) with its own set of environmental puzzles, or revisit completed worlds to find any [secret stickers](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/) you may have missed. And if you are playing on a device that struggles with the Mole Mines' effects-heavy areas, do not forget to check our [troubleshooting guide](/blog/troubleshooting-common-errors/) for solutions.

Tips for Mastering the Mole Mines

Mining Cart Survival Tips

Memorize obstacle patterns. Each cart section has a fixed layout, so repeated runs let you learn the sequence. Focus on survival first, then optimize for coin collection on subsequent runs.

When approaching jumps over gaps, keep in mind that the cart has slight forward momentum. Jump a tiny bit earlier than you think you need to. Late jumps are the number one cause of cart section failures.

Gem Puzzle Tips

Keep track of which gems you have collected. The game does show your gem inventory in the pause menu, but it is easy to forget which puzzles you have already solved. If you are missing a gem and cannot remember which one, the friendly mole NPC in Area 5 gives hints about gem locations.

Combat Tips in Darkness

In the Deep Tunnels, use the ground pound attack frequently. Its area-of-effect damage can hit mole enemies you cannot see in the darkness. The shockwave from a ground pound briefly illuminates a larger area, acting as a sort of sonar.

Performance Tips

The Mole Mines has heavy lighting and particle effects, especially in the Crystal Caverns. If you experience frame drops or lag, our lag fix and optimization guide covers settings adjustments that can help without sacrificing too much visual quality.

Connection to Other Worlds

The Mole Mines connects to the broader Super Bear Adventure story in several ways. The Crystal Crown from the Mole King is required for one of the final overworld missions, which ties together items from multiple worlds. This mission chain is covered in our all missions guide.

Some tunnels in the Mole Mines have visual connections to other worlds. One Deep Tunnel section has a glass wall looking into what appears to be the underground portion of The Hive, suggesting the two worlds are geographically connected in the game’s lore. For more on the game’s story and world connections, see our lore and story guide.

The Mole Mines cart mechanics also appear in a mini-game in the Arcade World, where you race through a procedurally generated track for a high score.

Conclusion

The Mole Mines offers one of the most varied and exciting experiences in Super Bear Adventure. Between the adrenaline of mining cart rides, the satisfaction of solving gem puzzles, and the tension of navigating dark tunnels, this world has something for every type of player. Use this walkthrough to find every secret, solve every puzzle, and defeat the Mole King with confidence.

For your next challenge, consider tackling the Beemothep Desert with its own set of environmental puzzles, or revisit completed worlds to find any secret stickers you may have missed. And if you are playing on a device that struggles with the Mole Mines’ effects-heavy areas, do not forget to check our troubleshooting guide for solutions.

How to Install Super Bear Adventure Mod APK Without Losing Progress
Install Super Bear Adventure Mod APK over the official version or an older mod without losing your save data, coins, or unlocked skins. Full guide with troubleshooting.
Will installing the mod APK delete my progress?
No, if you do NOT uninstall the existing game first. Install over the top (update install) and Android preserves the save data directory. Only an uninstall or factory reset deletes save data.
Can I go from the official game to the mod APK without losing saves?
Yes — if both the official game and mod APK use the same package name (com.earthkwak.superbearadventure), Android will treat the mod APK as an update. Your data transfers automatically.
What if Android says the install is blocked because signatures don't match?
This happens when the official Google Play version and the mod APK have different signing keys. In this case, you must uninstall the official version first. Backup your save data before uninstalling.
Where is the Super Bear Adventure save file stored?
Save data is in /Android/data/[package name]/ on your internal storage. You need a file manager with root or Android 11+ Documents access to see and copy this folder.
Can I switch between official and mod APK and keep my data?
If the signature conflict block applies, switching requires uninstall. Backup your save data before switching direction.
Install the mod APK over an existing version while keeping all save data
Before any APK change, copy your save data. The save file is at /Android/data/[game package name]/. Copy this folder to your SD card or Google Drive.
Download the new APK without uninstalling
Download the mod APK from superbearadventuremod.io/download/. Do not uninstall the existing game — your save data lives in that package folder.
Install the APK (update, not fresh install)
Tap the downloaded APK. Android will show 'Update app?' if the same package name is detected. Tap OK/Install.
After install, launch the game. Your coins, worlds, skins, and progress should be exactly where you left them.
If data is missing, restore from backup
If progress is gone, close the game. Copy your backup folder back to /Android/data/[package]/. Relaunch — progress restored.

Installing the Super Bear Adventure Mod APK does not automatically erase your progress — but the outcome depends on how you install it. This guide covers the correct approach to preserve all your save data.

## The Key Rule: Do NOT Uninstall First

The most important step is this: **do not uninstall the existing game before installing the mod APK**.

Android stores save data in a per-package directory:
```
/Android/data/[package name]/files/
```

This directory is **deleted when you uninstall the app** and **preserved when you update (install-over) the app**. As long as the mod APK has the same package name as the version you are replacing, Android will perform an update-in-place and keep the save directory intact.

## When Install-Over Works Seamlessly

**Updating mod APK to newer mod version:**
If you have our mod APK installed and you download a newer version from [/download/](/download/), install it directly. Android detects the same package name and same signing key → performs a clean update → saves preserved.

**Updating within the same signing key family:**
If the package name matches and the signing certificates are compatible, install-over works and data is kept.

## When You Must Uninstall First

**Official Google Play version → Mod APK:**

Google Play signs apps with Google's managed signing key. Mod APKs are signed with a different key (the mod developer's key). When the signing keys differ, Android blocks the install-over with the error:

> "An existing package by the same name with a conflicting signature is already installed."

In this case, you **must** uninstall the official version first. This means save data in the app's private directory will be lost unless you back it up.

**Backup procedure before uninstalling the official version:**

1. Install a file manager that supports Android/data access (see below)
2. Navigate to `/Android/data/[package name]/`
3. Copy the entire folder to your SD card, Google Drive, or PC
4. Uninstall the official game
5. Install the mod APK
6. Copy the save folder back to `/Android/data/[package name]/`
7. Launch — progress restored

## How to Back Up Save Data

### Method 1: File Manager (Android 11+)

Android 11 introduced restrictions on `/Android/data/` access. To copy save files:

1. Install **Cx File Explorer** or **ZArchiver** (these support the required SAF access)
2. Navigate to Internal Storage → Android → data
3. Grant "Allow access to Android" permission when prompted
4. Find the Super Bear Adventure package folder
5. Copy the folder to a safe location

### Method 2: Google Drive (Automatic)

If you have Android Auto Backup enabled (most phones, enabled by default):

1. Go to Settings → Google → Backup → ensure "Back up to Google Drive" is ON
2. The game's save data may be included (depends on whether the developer opted in)
3. After reinstalling, sign in to Google on the new install to trigger restore

Note: Not all games support Google Drive save backup — depends on the app.

### Method 3: ADB Backup (Advanced)

Connect your phone to a PC with USB Debugging enabled:

```
adb backup -noapk com.earthkwak.superbearadventure
```

Restore with:

```
adb restore backup.ab
```

This is the most reliable method but requires ADB setup.

## Updating from an Older Mod Version

If you currently have an older mod APK and want to update to v12.1.5:

1. Download v12.1.5 from [superbearadventuremod.io/download/](/download/)
2. Tap the APK file — Android shows "Update app?"
3. Tap Install — update installs in place
4. Launch — progress, coins, and unlocks are preserved

This is the most common scenario and it works automatically.

## Verifying Progress After Install

After installing (or updating), launch the game and check:

- Coin balance is intact
- Skins and hats are still unlocked
- World progress (completed missions) is saved
- Achievement progress carries over

If any of these are missing, the save data was not preserved — restore from backup.

## Common Error Messages and Fixes

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| "Conflicting signature" | Official Play version vs mod APK signing mismatch | Backup save data → uninstall official → install mod |
| "App not installed" | Insufficient storage | Free up 500+ MB |
| "Parse error" | Corrupted download | Re-download from [/download/](/download/) |
| Progress missing after install | Data loss during update | Restore from backup |

- [Save data backup guide](/blog/super-bear-adventure-save-data-backup/)
- [Update guide (mod to mod)](/blog/update-super-bear-adventure-mod/)
- [Download page](/download/)
- [Install guide for all devices](/install/)
