Super Bear Adventure Mod Menu Guide - All Features & How to Use
Complete guide to the Super Bear Adventure mod menu. Learn every mod menu feature, how to activate options, best settings, and pro tips for gameplay.

## What Is the Super Bear Adventure Mod Menu?

The mod menu is a powerful in-game overlay included with the [Super Bear Adventure Mod APK](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-apk-download/) that gives you access to a wide range of gameplay modifications. Think of it as a control panel that lets you customize your gameplay experience in real time, toggling features on and off as you see fit.

Unlike the standard game where you must work within the intended design constraints, the mod menu puts you in complete control. Want to explore a dangerous area without worrying about enemies? Toggle on God Mode. Need to reach a distant platform? Increase your jump height. Curious about what is behind a wall? Enable No Clip and walk right through it.

This guide covers every feature available in the mod menu, explains how to use each one effectively, and provides tips for getting the most out of your modded experience. If you have not installed the mod yet, visit our [main download page](/blog/super-bear-adventure-mod-apk-download/) for the latest version and installation instructions.

## How to Access the Mod Menu

![Mod menu interface layout](/images/guides/mod-menu-interface.svg)

### Opening the Menu

The mod menu is accessed through a floating button that appears on your screen during gameplay. Here is how to find and use it:

1. **Launch the game** after installing the mod APK
2. **Look for the floating icon** in the corner of your screen. It is typically a small circular button that can be dragged to any position
3. **Tap the floating icon** to open the mod menu overlay
4. **Browse through the options** using the scrollable menu interface
5. **Toggle features** on or off by tapping the corresponding switches
6. **Close the menu** by tapping the floating icon again or tapping outside the menu area

### Repositioning the Menu Button

The floating menu button can be moved to any position on your screen:

- **Long press** the floating icon
- **Drag it** to your preferred location
- **Release** to set its new position

This is useful for placing the button where it does not interfere with gameplay controls but remains easily accessible when you need it.

### Menu Interface Layout

The mod menu is organized into categories for easy navigation:

- **Player Modifications**: Features that affect Baaren's abilities
- **World Modifications**: Features that affect the game environment
- **Combat Modifications**: Features that affect fighting and enemies
- **Visual Modifications**: Features that change visual elements
- **Utility Features**: Convenience tools and shortcuts

## Player Modification Features

![Mod features list overview](/images/guides/mod-features-list.svg)

### God Mode (Invincibility)

**What it does**: Makes Baaren completely immune to all damage sources, including enemy attacks, environmental hazards, fall damage, and boss attacks.

**How to use it effectively**:
- Toggle on when exploring dangerous areas for the first time to learn layouts without frustration
- Perfect for practicing [boss fight](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/) patterns without the risk of dying
- Useful when exploring [secret areas](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/) that are heavily guarded
- Ideal for completionists who want to focus on finding [stickers](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/) without combat interruptions

**Tips**:
- God Mode does not prevent knockback effects, so you can still be pushed off platforms by strong attacks
- Consider disabling God Mode for boss fights once you have learned the patterns, as defeating bosses legitimately is more satisfying
- In [multiplayer](/blog/multiplayer-guide/), using God Mode may diminish the cooperative experience for other players

### Speed Modification

**What it does**: Allows you to adjust Baaren's movement speed with a slider ranging from 0.5x (half speed) to 5x (five times normal speed).

**Speed settings and their uses**:

| Speed Setting | Best Use Case |
|--------------|---------------|
| 0.5x | Precise platforming, detailed exploration |
| 1.0x | Normal gameplay (default) |
| 1.5x | General exploration, moderate traversal boost |
| 2.0x | Quick backtracking through known areas |
| 3.0x | Speedrunning practice, long-distance travel |
| 5.0x | Rapid world traversal, testing routes |

**Tips**:
- Higher speeds make precise platforming more difficult. Reduce speed when approaching tricky jump sequences
- Speed modification stacks with ice physics in [Snow Valley](/blog/snow-valley-secrets/), which can make control challenging at high speeds
- Using moderate speed boosts (1.5x-2x) is often more practical than maximum speed since it improves travel time without sacrificing too much control
- Great for [time trial medal practice](/blog/get-all-medals/) by learning optimal routes at normal speed, then testing whether your route can meet gold requirements

### Jump Enhancement

**What it does**: Modifies Baaren's jumping capabilities with several sub-options:

- **Jump Height Multiplier**: Increases the height of each jump (1x to 10x)
- **Infinite Double Jump**: Allows unlimited mid-air jumps instead of just one double jump
- **Float Mode**: Hold the jump button to float slowly downward after jumping

**How to use it effectively**:
- Infinite double jump is incredibly useful for reaching [secret areas](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/) and exploring vertical spaces
- Moderate jump height increases (2x-3x) help with platforming without being too extreme
- Float mode is perfect for precision landings on small platforms
- Combine with speed modification for efficient aerial exploration of large worlds

**Tips**:
- Extremely high jump height (5x+) can launch you out of the playable area. Use with caution
- Infinite double jump trivializes most platforming challenges, so consider using it only for exploration rather than regular gameplay
- Float mode works well in the [Giant House](/blog/giant-house-walkthrough/) where falling distances are significant

### No Clip (Walk Through Walls)

**What it does**: Disables collision detection with walls, floors, and obstacles, allowing Baaren to pass through any solid object.

**How to use it effectively**:
- Explore areas behind walls and under floors to find hidden content
- Skip locked doors or barriers that normally require puzzle solutions
- Move through the ground to discover underground areas or hidden rooms
- Access developer areas or out-of-bounds regions (if any exist)

**Tips**:
- Always note your position before using No Clip, as you can easily become disoriented when passing through solid objects
- Disable No Clip before the ground beneath you disappears, or you may fall indefinitely
- No Clip can reveal how game worlds are constructed, which some players find interesting from a game design perspective
- Be careful using this in the [Mole Mines](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/) as underground collision geometry can be complex

### Teleportation

**What it does**: Allows instant travel to specific locations marked on the map or to coordinates you set manually.

**Teleportation modes**:

- **Map Teleport**: Open the world map, tap any location, and instantly travel there
- **Bookmark Teleport**: Save up to 10 custom locations and teleport to them at any time
- **NPC Teleport**: Instantly travel to the location of any known NPC
- **Spawn Teleport**: Return to the world's spawn point instantly

**Tips**:
- Bookmark useful locations like shopkeepers, quest NPCs, and secret area entrances
- Map Teleport works across different world sections, making it faster than running
- NPC Teleport is helpful when you need to turn in quests or buy items
- Combined with [unlimited money](/blog/super-bear-adventure-unlimited-money/), teleportation to shops lets you quickly purchase and try new cosmetics

## Combat Modification Features

![Combat basics in Super Bear Adventure](/images/gameplay/combat-basics.svg)

### One-Hit Kill

**What it does**: Every attack from Baaren defeats any enemy or boss in a single hit, regardless of the target's normal health pool.

**How to use it effectively**:
- Clear enemy-heavy areas quickly when your goal is exploration rather than combat
- Useful for farming enemy-dropped items without time-consuming fights
- Speeds up [mission](/blog/all-missions-guide/) completion when combat segments are repetitive
- Can be combined with God Mode for completely stress-free gameplay

**Tips**:
- Using One-Hit Kill on bosses removes the challenge and spectacle of multi-phase boss fights, so consider saving it for repeat encounters rather than first-time boss meetings
- Some [medal challenges](/blog/get-all-medals/) specifically require defeating enemies under certain conditions, and One-Hit Kill may interfere with these

### Freeze Enemies

**What it does**: All enemies in the current area stop moving and cannot attack. They become stationary obstacles that can be defeated normally or simply ignored.

**How to use it effectively**:
- Create a safe environment for thorough exploration
- Study enemy positions and patrol routes before engaging them normally
- Take screenshots of enemy placements for guide creation
- Practice platforming sections near enemies without combat pressure

**Tips**:
- Frozen enemies still have their collision boxes, so they can block paths
- Unfreezing enemies returns them to their normal behavior immediately
- Boss enemies may require a brief moment to re-sync their animations when unfrozen

### Unlimited Stamina

**What it does**: Prevents stamina or energy from depleting, allowing unlimited use of stamina-based actions like sprinting, special attacks, and sustained abilities.

**Tips**:
- This feature is most noticeable in areas where extended sprinting is needed
- Combine with speed modification for maximum traversal efficiency
- Useful in timed challenges where stamina management would normally slow you down

## World Modification Features

### Auto-Collect

**What it does**: Automatically collects coins, items, and pickups within a configurable radius around Baaren. The collection radius can be set from small (nearby items only) to large (items across a wide area).

**Radius settings**:
- **Small**: Collects items within arm's reach (slight convenience boost)
- **Medium**: Collects items within a moderate area (good for general play)
- **Large**: Collects items within a wide radius (efficient for coin farming)
- **Maximum**: Collects all items in the current area regardless of distance

**Tips**:
- Combined with [unlimited money](/blog/super-bear-adventure-unlimited-money/), auto-collect mostly serves as a convenience feature since coins are already infinite
- Auto-collect picks up stickers and special items too, which is helpful for [sticker hunting](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/)
- Maximum radius can cause performance issues on older devices since it processes many items simultaneously
- Does not automatically collect items that require specific interaction (like quest items that need a button press)

### Weather and Time Control

**What it does**: Allows you to change the current weather conditions and time of day in any world.

**Options**:
- Clear skies, rain, snow, fog, or storm weather
- Dawn, morning, noon, afternoon, sunset, or night time settings
- Static time (prevent time of day from changing)

**Tips**:
- Changing weather can reveal hidden items that only appear in specific conditions
- Night mode creates atmospheric exploration experiences
- Clear skies at noon provides the best visibility for finding hidden objects
- Snow weather in non-snow worlds creates amusing visual combinations

## Visual Modification Features

### Camera Options

**What it does**: Provides advanced camera controls beyond the standard game settings:

- **Zoom Range**: Expand the zoom-in and zoom-out range significantly
- **Free Camera**: Detach the camera from Baaren to explore the world independently
- **First Person Mode**: Switch to a first-person perspective
- **Cinematic Mode**: Widen the field of view for a more cinematic appearance

**Tips**:
- Free camera is excellent for scouting ahead in dangerous areas

What Is the Super Bear Adventure Mod Menu?

The mod menu is a powerful in-game overlay included with the Super Bear Adventure Mod APK that gives you access to a wide range of gameplay modifications. Think of it as a control panel that lets you customize your gameplay experience in real time, toggling features on and off as you see fit.

Unlike the standard game where you must work within the intended design constraints, the mod menu puts you in complete control. Want to explore a dangerous area without worrying about enemies? Toggle on God Mode. Need to reach a distant platform? Increase your jump height. Curious about what is behind a wall? Enable No Clip and walk right through it.

This guide covers every feature available in the mod menu, explains how to use each one effectively, and provides tips for getting the most out of your modded experience. If you have not installed the mod yet, visit our main download page for the latest version and installation instructions.

How to Access the Mod Menu

Opening the Menu

The mod menu is accessed through a floating button that appears on your screen during gameplay. Here is how to find and use it:

  1. Launch the game after installing the mod APK
  2. Look for the floating icon in the corner of your screen. It is typically a small circular button that can be dragged to any position
  3. Tap the floating icon to open the mod menu overlay
  4. Browse through the options using the scrollable menu interface
  5. Toggle features on or off by tapping the corresponding switches
  6. Close the menu by tapping the floating icon again or tapping outside the menu area

Repositioning the Menu Button

The floating menu button can be moved to any position on your screen:

  • Long press the floating icon
  • Drag it to your preferred location
  • Release to set its new position

This is useful for placing the button where it does not interfere with gameplay controls but remains easily accessible when you need it.

Menu Interface Layout

The mod menu is organized into categories for easy navigation:

  • Player Modifications: Features that affect Baaren’s abilities
  • World Modifications: Features that affect the game environment
  • Combat Modifications: Features that affect fighting and enemies
  • Visual Modifications: Features that change visual elements
  • Utility Features: Convenience tools and shortcuts

Player Modification Features

God Mode (Invincibility)

What it does: Makes Baaren completely immune to all damage sources, including enemy attacks, environmental hazards, fall damage, and boss attacks.

How to use it effectively:

  • Toggle on when exploring dangerous areas for the first time to learn layouts without frustration
  • Perfect for practicing boss fight patterns without the risk of dying
  • Useful when exploring secret areas that are heavily guarded
  • Ideal for completionists who want to focus on finding stickers without combat interruptions

Tips:

  • God Mode does not prevent knockback effects, so you can still be pushed off platforms by strong attacks
  • Consider disabling God Mode for boss fights once you have learned the patterns, as defeating bosses legitimately is more satisfying
  • In multiplayer, using God Mode may diminish the cooperative experience for other players

Speed Modification

What it does: Allows you to adjust Baaren’s movement speed with a slider ranging from 0.5x (half speed) to 5x (five times normal speed).

Speed settings and their uses:

Speed SettingBest Use Case
0.5xPrecise platforming, detailed exploration
1.0xNormal gameplay (default)
1.5xGeneral exploration, moderate traversal boost
2.0xQuick backtracking through known areas
3.0xSpeedrunning practice, long-distance travel
5.0xRapid world traversal, testing routes

Tips:

  • Higher speeds make precise platforming more difficult. Reduce speed when approaching tricky jump sequences
  • Speed modification stacks with ice physics in Snow Valley, which can make control challenging at high speeds
  • Using moderate speed boosts (1.5x-2x) is often more practical than maximum speed since it improves travel time without sacrificing too much control
  • Great for time trial medal practice by learning optimal routes at normal speed, then testing whether your route can meet gold requirements

Jump Enhancement

What it does: Modifies Baaren’s jumping capabilities with several sub-options:

  • Jump Height Multiplier: Increases the height of each jump (1x to 10x)
  • Infinite Double Jump: Allows unlimited mid-air jumps instead of just one double jump
  • Float Mode: Hold the jump button to float slowly downward after jumping

How to use it effectively:

  • Infinite double jump is incredibly useful for reaching secret areas and exploring vertical spaces
  • Moderate jump height increases (2x-3x) help with platforming without being too extreme
  • Float mode is perfect for precision landings on small platforms
  • Combine with speed modification for efficient aerial exploration of large worlds

Tips:

  • Extremely high jump height (5x+) can launch you out of the playable area. Use with caution
  • Infinite double jump trivializes most platforming challenges, so consider using it only for exploration rather than regular gameplay
  • Float mode works well in the Giant House where falling distances are significant

No Clip (Walk Through Walls)

What it does: Disables collision detection with walls, floors, and obstacles, allowing Baaren to pass through any solid object.

How to use it effectively:

  • Explore areas behind walls and under floors to find hidden content
  • Skip locked doors or barriers that normally require puzzle solutions
  • Move through the ground to discover underground areas or hidden rooms
  • Access developer areas or out-of-bounds regions (if any exist)

Tips:

  • Always note your position before using No Clip, as you can easily become disoriented when passing through solid objects
  • Disable No Clip before the ground beneath you disappears, or you may fall indefinitely
  • No Clip can reveal how game worlds are constructed, which some players find interesting from a game design perspective
  • Be careful using this in the Mole Mines as underground collision geometry can be complex

Teleportation

What it does: Allows instant travel to specific locations marked on the map or to coordinates you set manually.

Teleportation modes:

  • Map Teleport: Open the world map, tap any location, and instantly travel there
  • Bookmark Teleport: Save up to 10 custom locations and teleport to them at any time
  • NPC Teleport: Instantly travel to the location of any known NPC
  • Spawn Teleport: Return to the world’s spawn point instantly

Tips:

  • Bookmark useful locations like shopkeepers, quest NPCs, and secret area entrances
  • Map Teleport works across different world sections, making it faster than running
  • NPC Teleport is helpful when you need to turn in quests or buy items
  • Combined with unlimited money, teleportation to shops lets you quickly purchase and try new cosmetics

Combat Modification Features

One-Hit Kill

What it does: Every attack from Baaren defeats any enemy or boss in a single hit, regardless of the target’s normal health pool.

How to use it effectively:

  • Clear enemy-heavy areas quickly when your goal is exploration rather than combat
  • Useful for farming enemy-dropped items without time-consuming fights
  • Speeds up mission completion when combat segments are repetitive
  • Can be combined with God Mode for completely stress-free gameplay

Tips:

  • Using One-Hit Kill on bosses removes the challenge and spectacle of multi-phase boss fights, so consider saving it for repeat encounters rather than first-time boss meetings
  • Some medal challenges specifically require defeating enemies under certain conditions, and One-Hit Kill may interfere with these

Freeze Enemies

What it does: All enemies in the current area stop moving and cannot attack. They become stationary obstacles that can be defeated normally or simply ignored.

How to use it effectively:

  • Create a safe environment for thorough exploration
  • Study enemy positions and patrol routes before engaging them normally
  • Take screenshots of enemy placements for guide creation
  • Practice platforming sections near enemies without combat pressure

Tips:

  • Frozen enemies still have their collision boxes, so they can block paths
  • Unfreezing enemies returns them to their normal behavior immediately
  • Boss enemies may require a brief moment to re-sync their animations when unfrozen

Unlimited Stamina

What it does: Prevents stamina or energy from depleting, allowing unlimited use of stamina-based actions like sprinting, special attacks, and sustained abilities.

Tips:

  • This feature is most noticeable in areas where extended sprinting is needed
  • Combine with speed modification for maximum traversal efficiency
  • Useful in timed challenges where stamina management would normally slow you down

World Modification Features

Auto-Collect

What it does: Automatically collects coins, items, and pickups within a configurable radius around Baaren. The collection radius can be set from small (nearby items only) to large (items across a wide area).

Radius settings:

  • Small: Collects items within arm’s reach (slight convenience boost)
  • Medium: Collects items within a moderate area (good for general play)
  • Large: Collects items within a wide radius (efficient for coin farming)
  • Maximum: Collects all items in the current area regardless of distance

Tips:

  • Combined with unlimited money, auto-collect mostly serves as a convenience feature since coins are already infinite
  • Auto-collect picks up stickers and special items too, which is helpful for sticker hunting
  • Maximum radius can cause performance issues on older devices since it processes many items simultaneously
  • Does not automatically collect items that require specific interaction (like quest items that need a button press)

Weather and Time Control

What it does: Allows you to change the current weather conditions and time of day in any world.

Options:

  • Clear skies, rain, snow, fog, or storm weather
  • Dawn, morning, noon, afternoon, sunset, or night time settings
  • Static time (prevent time of day from changing)

Tips:

  • Changing weather can reveal hidden items that only appear in specific conditions
  • Night mode creates atmospheric exploration experiences
  • Clear skies at noon provides the best visibility for finding hidden objects
  • Snow weather in non-snow worlds creates amusing visual combinations

How to Backup & Restore Super Bear Adventure Save Data (Android 2026)
Complete guide to backing up and restoring Super Bear Adventure save data on Android. Protect your progress before updating, reinstalling, or switching to the mod APK.
Where is Super Bear Adventure save data stored on Android?
Save data is stored in /Android/data/[package name]/ on your device's internal storage. On Android 11+, you need a compatible file manager or ADB to access this folder.
How do I backup Super Bear Adventure save data?
Use a file manager with Android/data access (Cx File Explorer, ZArchiver), copy the game's data folder to SD card or cloud storage, then restore it after reinstalling.
Can I backup save data to Google Drive?
Android's Auto Backup may include save data if the game developer enabled it. For guaranteed backup, use manual file copy via file manager or ADB.
Does updating the mod APK delete save data?
No — if you install the new APK over the existing one (do not uninstall first), save data is preserved. Only uninstalling the app deletes save data.
Can I transfer save data between devices?
Yes. Copy the save data folder from your old device, transfer the folder to your new device, install the APK, and copy the folder to the same location.
How to Backup Super Bear Adventure Save Data on Android
Step-by-step backup and restore guide for Android save data
Install Cx File Explorer or ZArchiver from Google Play. These apps support access to the Android/data/ folder where save data is stored.
Open the file manager, navigate to Internal Storage → Android → data. When prompted, grant 'Allow access to Android' via the Android system dialog.
Locate the Super Bear Adventure package folder under Android/data/. Copy the entire folder.
Paste the copied folder to your SD card (Create/Games/SBABackup/) or a cloud storage folder (Google Drive, Dropbox).
After reinstalling, before launching the game, copy the backup folder back to Android/data/. Launch the game — your progress is restored.

Losing game progress is frustrating. Whether you are updating to a new mod APK version, switching devices, or doing a factory reset, backing up your Super Bear Adventure save data takes less than 5 minutes and saves hours of re-grinding.

## Why Backup Matters Especially for Mod APK Users

Save data loss is a particular risk when:

1. **Uninstalling to switch from official to mod APK** — the signature mismatch requires uninstall, which deletes data
2. **Factory resetting your device** — everything is wiped including game data
3. **Switching to a new phone** — transfer requires manual copy
4. **A rare APK corruption** — corrupt install can wipe the save directory

The [mod APK update process](/blog/update-super-bear-adventure-mod/) (updating without uninstalling) preserves data automatically. But when an uninstall is needed, you need a backup.

## Understanding Save Data Location

Android apps store private data in:
```
/Internal Storage/Android/data/[package name]/
```

For Super Bear Adventure, the package name is the app's unique identifier (e.g., `com.earthkwak.superbearadventure`). The save files are inside this directory.

**Access restriction:** Android 11 (API 30) added restrictions on accessing other apps' `/Android/data/` folders. Standard file managers no longer see this directory. You need a file manager that uses the Storage Access Framework (SAF) or ADB.

## Method 1: File Manager with SAF (Recommended for Most Users)

**Compatible apps:** Cx File Explorer (free), ZArchiver (free), Solid Explorer (paid)

### Backup Steps

1. Install **Cx File Explorer** from Google Play
2. Open Cx File Explorer
3. Navigate to **Internal Storage → Android → data**
4. Cx File Explorer will show a system dialog: "Allow Cx File Explorer to access this folder?" — tap **Allow**
5. Find the Super Bear Adventure package folder
6. Long-press the folder → **Copy**
7. Navigate to **SD Card** (or create a folder like `/Games/SBABackup/`)
8. **Paste** the folder
9. Done — backup complete

### Restore Steps

1. Open Cx File Explorer
2. Navigate to your backup location and copy the saved folder
3. Navigate to **Internal Storage → Android → data**
4. Paste the folder here
5. If prompted to replace, confirm replacement
6. **Important:** Do this BEFORE launching the game for the first time after reinstall
7. Launch Super Bear Adventure — progress restored

## Method 2: ADB Backup (Most Reliable, Requires PC)

ADB (Android Debug Bridge) is Google's developer tool. This method works on Android 11+ without any file manager workarounds.

### Requirements

- Android Studio installed on PC, or ADB standalone tools
- USB cable
- Developer Options enabled on your phone (Settings → About Phone → tap Build Number 7 times)
- USB Debugging enabled in Developer Options

### Backup Command

Connect your phone to PC via USB. In a terminal:

```bash
adb backup -noapk com.earthkwak.superbearadventure -f sba_save_backup.ab
```

This creates `sba_save_backup.ab` on your PC — your save data archive.

### Restore Command

After reinstalling the APK:

```bash
adb restore sba_save_backup.ab
```

Confirm the restore on your phone when prompted.

## Method 3: Google Drive Auto Backup

Android's Auto Backup feature automatically backs up app data to Google Drive. Whether your progress is included depends on whether Earthkwak Games opted their save data into the backup scheme.

**To check if it is working:**

1. Settings → Google → Backup → Last backup time (should be recent)
2. Uninstall and reinstall the game
3. Sign in with your Google account when prompted
4. If backup data exists, Android will offer to restore it

**Limitation:** This is not guaranteed for mod APK installs, as the different app signature may not trigger the standard backup association. Use Method 1 or 2 for reliable backup.

## Transferring Save Data Between Devices

To move your progress to a new Android phone:

1. On old device: Copy the save folder to Google Drive or a shared SD card
2. On new device: Install the mod APK from [/download/](/download/)
3. Do NOT launch the game yet
4. Copy the save folder from Drive/SD to `/Android/data/[package name]/` on the new device
5. Launch the game — progress appears

For the same phone: [Installing mod APK without losing progress](/blog/install-apk-without-uninstalling-original/)

## Before Every Update: Quick Backup Checklist

1. Open Cx File Explorer
2. Copy `/Android/data/[game package]/` to SD card
3. Then download and install the new APK

Takes 2 minutes. Saves all your coins, skins, and world progress.

Download the latest mod APK from [/download/](/download/) when ready to update.

Super Bear Fighters is the PvP mini-game added to Super Bear Adventure in v12.1.0 (November 2025). Located in the Arcade World, it is a fully functional fighting game mode where Baaren faces off against other players' bears in 1v1 and 2v2 matches.

## What Is Super Bear Fighters?

Super Bear Fighters takes the bear character models from Super Bear Adventure and puts them in a dedicated fighting arena. It is inspired by classic fighting games but simplified for casual play:

- 2D arena fighting on scrolling stages
- Health bars for both players
- Special moves unique to each bear skin
- Ranked matchmaking with seasonal rewards
- 1v1 and 2v2 game modes

This is not a separate game — it lives inside Super Bear Adventure's Arcade World and is accessible from the main game.

## How to Access Super Bear Fighters

1. From the Super Bear Adventure main menu, select **World Select**
2. Choose **Arcade World**
3. In the Arcade World, look for the Super Bear Fighters arena (large illuminated cabinet near the center)
4. Walk up to it and press the Interact button
5. Select 1v1 or 2v2, then Online Match or Local Match

**Requirement:** At least one world boss must be defeated to unlock the Arcade World.

## Game Modes

### 1v1 Online

Standard competitive mode. Matched against random opponents from the global player pool. Wins earn ranking points (see Ranking section).

### 2v2 Online

Team mode. Requires a partner from your friend list or current co-op session. Fight against another team of 2. Team matches earn 1.5x ranking points compared to 1v1.

### Local Match

Fight against a friend in the same co-op session. No ranking points are earned but it is great for practice. Both players need to be in the same multiplayer lobby. See the [multiplayer guide](/blog/multiplayer-guide/) for lobby setup.

### Training Mode

Practice mode with a stationary AI opponent. No ranking, no penalties. The only mode that works fully offline.

## Characters and Moves

Each bear skin in Super Bear Adventure has a slightly different moveset in Super Bear Fighters. The base moveset is the same for all bears but each has one unique special:

### Base Moveset (All Bears)

| Input | Move |
|-------|------|
| A/D (left/right) | Move |
| W / Tap Jump | Jump |
| Tap Attack | Jab (fast, low damage) |
| Hold Attack + release | Charged Strike (slow, high damage) |
| Down + Attack while airborne | Pounce (slam, good range) |
| Double tap direction | Dash |
| Dash + Attack | Dash Strike (moves forward, medium damage) |

### Special Moves by Skin

**Standard Baaren:** Spin Attack — 360-degree AoE around Baaren, hits both sides.

**Skeleton Bear:** Bone Toss — throws a projectile straight ahead. Only projectile in the game.

**Royal Cape:** Cape Slash — dramatic downward slash, highest single-hit damage of any move.

**Christmas Baaren:** Gift Drop — drops a present that explodes after 2 seconds. Can be placed strategically.

The mod APK unlocks all skins (see [unlock guide](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/)), giving you access to all special moves from the start.

## Ranking System

Super Bear Fighters has a seasonal ranking ladder:

### Ranks

1. **Honey Cub** — starting rank
2. **Bee Catcher** — intermediate
3. **Forest Guardian** — advanced
4. **Alpha Bear** — elite
5. **Earthkwak Champion** — highest rank, top players per season

### Ranking Points

- 1v1 win: +20 points
- 1v1 loss: −10 points
- 2v2 win: +30 points
- 2v2 loss: −10 points

Promotion to the next rank requires reaching a point threshold. Demotion happens if you fall below the rank floor.

### Seasonal Rewards

Each season (approximately 3 months) awards cosmetic rewards based on your rank at season end:

- Honey Cub: Standard season sticker
- Bee Catcher: Special hat
- Forest Guardian: Rare skin tone variant
- Alpha Bear: Exclusive seasonal skin
- Earthkwak Champion: Animated crown accessory (rarest cosmetic in the game)

## Strategies to Win

### For Beginners

1. **Learn the Charged Strike timing** — this is your highest-damage reliable move. Most new players only jab, which does low damage. Mixing Charged Strikes into your neutral game massively increases damage output.

2. **Use the Pounce attack** — the airborne pounce has the largest hitbox of any grounded move. Jump, then pounce to start most combos.

3. **Stay near the center** — falling off the arena edges results in ring-out (instant KO in some stages). Stay center until you learn stage layouts.

### Intermediate Play

1. **Match your skin's special to your playstyle:**
   - Skeleton Bear Bone Toss for zoning (keep distance)
   - Standard Baaren Spin for escape (use when surrounded)
   - Royal Cape slash for punishing dodges

2. **Watch for Dash Attack opportunities** — when opponents commit to a Charged Strike animation (it has a wind-up), dash in for a free punish.

3. **In 2v2, focus one target** — split damage between two opponents is inefficient. Both teammates should target the same opponent for faster KOs.

### Advanced

1. **Pounce-cancel combo:** Jump → Pounce → land → immediately Jab → Charged Strike. This 3-hit combo deals significant damage and the timing is fast enough that many opponents cannot interrupt it.

2. **Stage awareness:** Some Arcade World stages have moving platforms. Plan your movement around platform timing, not just opponent position.

3. **Read opponent specials:** Once you recognize which skin your opponent is using, you know their special move. Skeleton Bear players will try to zone — get in close. Royal Cape players will bait you to attack — bait their big swing instead.

## Frequently Asked Questions

## Method 4: World Completion Milestones

Completing all missions in a world triggers a completion milestone bonus that includes a one-time large purple gem reward:

- Each world: 50–100 purple gems on 100% completion
- All 6 worlds: 300–600 total gems from milestones alone

This is a one-time reward per account. After collecting these, you need to rely on other farming methods.

- Takes about 3 minutes per session
- Lower variance than other machines

- Up to 10 gems on high-score runs
- Requires skill but highest potential yield
- Gem rewards scale with score multiplier

**Best approach:** Play the Arcade World machines during daily rotations when gem bonuses are active. See the [Arcade World guide](/blog/arcade-world-mini-games/) for high score tips.

## Understanding the Coin Economy

### How Coins Work in the Standard Game

In the original Super Bear Adventure, coins serve as the primary currency. Players earn them through:

- **Mission completion**: Each [mission](/blog/all-missions-guide/) rewards a set number of coins upon completion
- **World exploration**: Coins are scattered throughout every world, placed on paths, hidden in secret areas, and tucked behind obstacles
- **Enemy defeats**: Some enemies drop coins when defeated
- **Challenges**: Timed challenges, combat challenges, and [medal achievements](/blog/get-all-medals/) award coin bonuses
- **Daily activities**: Daily login rewards and repeatable tasks provide steady coin income
- **Redeem codes**: Promotional [redeem codes](/blog/redeem-codes-list/) often include coin rewards

The standard coin economy is designed to create a gradual progression where players slowly save up for desired purchases. For many players, this grind becomes a barrier between them and the content they want to enjoy.

### How Unlimited Money Changes the Experience

With the unlimited money feature enabled in the mod APK, your coin balance is set to an extremely high number that functionally never decreases. When you make purchases, the amount is technically deducted, but the balance remains so large that it effectively never runs out.

This fundamentally changes the game experience by:

- Removing the waiting period between wanting and having

How Coins Work in the Standard Game

In the original Super Bear Adventure, coins serve as the primary currency. Players earn them through:

  • Mission completion: Each mission rewards a set number of coins upon completion
  • World exploration: Coins are scattered throughout every world, placed on paths, hidden in secret areas, and tucked behind obstacles
  • Enemy defeats: Some enemies drop coins when defeated
  • Challenges: Timed challenges, combat challenges, and medal achievements award coin bonuses
  • Daily activities: Daily login rewards and repeatable tasks provide steady coin income
  • Redeem codes: Promotional redeem codes often include coin rewards

The standard coin economy is designed to create a gradual progression where players slowly save up for desired purchases. For many players, this grind becomes a barrier between them and the content they want to enjoy.

Super Bear Adventure Vehicles — Every Rideable & How to Control Them (2026)
Complete guide to every vehicle and rideable in Super Bear Adventure. Scooter, sailboat, mine cart, glider, desert train, and more — where to find them and how to use them.
What vehicles are in Super Bear Adventure?

Super Bear Adventure includes: scooter (Turtletown), sailboat (beach area), mine cart (Mole Mines), glider (Snow Valley), desert train (Beemothep), and bee car (Giant House). Each handles differently.
How do you control the mine cart in Super Bear Adventure?
The mine cart moves automatically. Jump to switch tracks at split points, duck to avoid low-hanging obstacles, and lean to affect cart speed on inclined sections.
Where do you find the scooter in Super Bear Adventure?
The scooter is in Turtletown near the village market. It is one of the first vehicles you encounter and is required for a specific delivery mission.
Can you use vehicles in multiplayer?
Yes. In co-op sessions, each player can mount their own vehicle or ride together where vehicles support multiple passengers (like the sailboat).
Do vehicles have special tricks or shortcuts?
Yes. The speedrunning community has discovered tricks for several vehicles: the mine cart exit trick, the sailboat speed-boost maneuver, and the glider updraft technique.
How to Use Vehicles in Super Bear Adventure
Guide to finding, mounting, and controlling each vehicle
Each vehicle is located in a specific world. Check the guide below for exact locations in Turtletown, Snow Valley, Desert, Giant House, The Hive, and Mole Mines.
Approach the vehicle and press the Interact button when the prompt appears. Baaren automatically mounts.
Each vehicle has unique controls. Basic: left/right for steering, jump for jumping (where applicable), and the dedicated vehicle action button.
Press Interact or Jump to dismount, depending on the vehicle. For the mine cart, pressing jump while not jumping exits the cart.

Super Bear Adventure includes six distinct vehicles spread across its six worlds. Each vehicle has unique controls and is used in specific missions. This guide covers every rideable — where to find it, how to control it, and any known tricks.

## Complete Vehicle List

### 1. Scooter — Turtletown

**World:** Turtletown  
**Location:** Near the village market, parked beside the delivery stall  
**Used in:** Delivery missions in Turtletown

**Controls:**
- Left/Right: Steer
- Jump button: Hop (small hop, can mount curbs)
- Brake: Hold down while moving

**Tips:**
- The scooter is slower than running but required for specific delivery missions
- Stay on the main roads — the scooter handles poorly off-road
- Delivery timer missions (if any) require knowing the optimal route between delivery points

**Speedrun note:** The scooter delivery mission is one of the longer missions in Turtletown. Knowing the route in advance saves 30–60 seconds.

---

### 2. Sailboat — Turtletown Beach / Water Areas

**World:** Turtletown (beach section) and water areas in other worlds  
**Location:** Docked at the Turtletown beach marina  
**Used in:** Water crossing missions, ocean exploration

**Controls:**
- Left/Right: Steer direction
- Forward: Move forward automatically (press once to engage)
- Jump: Small hop (used to avoid waves)
- Dash: Speed boost (short duration)

**Tips:**
- The dash speed boost is activated by quickly pressing Forward twice. This is useful for racing missions
- The sailboat supports 2 players simultaneously in co-op — both players can ride on the same boat
- Hitting the dock at high speed causes a bounce effect that can launch Baaren off the boat

**Special technique — Sail skip:** On open water sections without mission triggers, the speed boost dash can be chained continuously to cross water areas significantly faster than normal sailing speed.

---

### 3. Glider — Snow Valley

**World:** Snow Valley  
**Location:** At the top of the first major cliff in Snow Valley, next to a launch pad  
**Used in:** Long-distance gap crossing, cliff descent, aerial exploration

**Controls:**
- Left/Right: Steer direction
- Forward/Back: Pitch (descend faster or slower)
- Jump button while on glider: Dismount (launches Baaren forward from glider)

**Tips:**
- The glider uses updraft currents — fly over visually distinct smoke/mist areas on the ground for altitude gain
- The glider provides the best aerial view of Snow Valley, useful for spotting sticker locations
- Glider dismount mid-air allows you to transition directly to Baaren's double jump for tricky precision landings

**Speedrun note:** The Snow Valley glider can reach areas that walking would take much longer to navigate. Knowing the updraft locations is key to glider speedrunning.

---

### 4. Desert Train — Beemothep Desert

**World:** Beemothep Desert  
**Location:** The desert train station on the western side of Beemothep  
**Used in:** Traversal across the wide desert sections, specific relic collection mission

**Controls:**
- Automatic movement (train follows rails)
- Left/Right: Lean to affect speed (lean forward = faster)
- Jump: Hop on/off the train
- Interact while on train: Trigger specific events (in mission)

**Tips:**
- The desert train has fixed stops — you cannot control its path
- Some collectibles (stickers) are visible from the train and can be grabbed by jumping off at the right moment
- The train is required for the main desert traversal mission and is optional for most other exploration

**Hidden feature:** At the final train stop, if you jump onto the roof of the station building before the train departs, you can access a hidden sticker that is otherwise very difficult to reach.

---

### 5. Bee Car — Giant House

**World:** Giant House  
**Location:** In the Giant House kitchen, parked beside the refrigerator  
**Used in:** Kitchen navigation mission, living room exploration

**Controls:**
- Left/Right: Steer
- Forward: Drive forward
- Jump: Hop (can jump onto countertops)
- Reverse: Hold back while at low speed

**Tips:**
- The Bee Car is sized appropriately for Baaren but is still quite large — some narrow passages require exiting the car
- The hop jump allows climbing onto kitchen counters, which is required for specific missions
- Multiple players can ride in the Bee Car in co-op (one front, one back)

**Unique mechanic:** The Bee Car's horn (press Interact while driving) triggers certain NPC reactions in the Giant House kitchen, which unlocks a hidden dialogue sequence.

---

### 6. Mine Cart — Mole Mines

**World:** Mole Mines  
**Location:** Available at the mine cart station at the start of Mole Mines  
**Used in:** Main traversal through the mine tunnel sections (required for main path)

**Controls:**
- Automatic forward movement
- Jump: Switch tracks at split points (jump right before the split)
- Duck: Avoid low-hanging stalactites
- Lean: Minor speed adjustment on inclines

**Tips:**
- Track switches happen fast — recognize the split point well in advance
- Jumping too early or too late at a split results in staying on the same track
- Some tracks lead to dead ends (dead-end tracks have extra coins but no exit — you loop back)
- The highest-gem-yield route in the mine cart takes the right track at split 2 and the left at split 4

**Speedrun trick — Cart Exit:** Exit the mine cart at split 1 by pressing Jump + Left simultaneously. This lands on a hidden ledge that bypasses the entire cart ride and emerges at the post-cart checkpoint. See the [speedrunning guide](/blog/speedrunning-super-bear-adventure/) for exact timing.

## Vehicle Comparison

| Vehicle | World | Control Style | Speed | Passengers |
|---------|-------|-------------|-------|-----------|
| Scooter | Turtletown | Steerable | Medium | 1 |
| Sailboat | Turtletown/Water | Steerable | Variable | 2 |
| Glider | Snow Valley | Pitch/Steer | Variable | 1 |
| Desert Train | Beemothep | Automatic | Fast | 1 |
| Bee Car | Giant House | Steerable | Slow | 2 |
| Mine Cart | Mole Mines | Automatic | Fast | 1 |

## Vehicles in Multiplayer

In co-op sessions, vehicles work as follows:

- **Scooter, Glider, Mine Cart, Desert Train:** Each player has their own instance. Multiple players can use vehicles simultaneously in the same area.
- **Sailboat, Bee Car:** Supports 2 players on the same vehicle. Player 1 controls, Player 2 rides.

For co-op setup, see the [multiplayer guide](/blog/multiplayer-guide/).

Special technique — Sail skip: On open water sections without mission triggers, the speed boost dash can be chained continuously to cross water areas significantly faster than normal sailing speed.

3. Glider — Snow Valley

World: Snow Valley
Location: At the top of the first major cliff in Snow Valley, next to a launch pad
Used in: Long-distance gap crossing, cliff descent, aerial exploration

Controls:

  • Left/Right: Steer direction
  • Forward/Back: Pitch (descend faster or slower)
  • Jump button while on glider: Dismount (launches Baaren forward from glider)

Tips:

  • The glider uses updraft currents — fly over visually distinct smoke/mist areas on the ground for altitude gain
  • The glider provides the best aerial view of Snow Valley, useful for spotting sticker locations
  • Glider dismount mid-air allows you to transition directly to Baaren’s double jump for tricky precision landings

Speedrun note: The Snow Valley glider can reach areas that walking would take much longer to navigate. Knowing the updraft locations is key to glider speedrunning.

4. Desert Train — Beemothep Desert

World: Beemothep Desert
Location: The desert train station on the western side of Beemothep
Used in: Traversal across the wide desert sections, specific relic collection mission

Controls:

  • Automatic movement (train follows rails)
  • Left/Right: Lean to affect speed (lean forward = faster)
  • Jump: Hop on/off the train
  • Interact while on train: Trigger specific events (in mission)

Tips:

  • The desert train has fixed stops — you cannot control its path
  • Some collectibles (stickers) are visible from the train and can be grabbed by jumping off at the right moment
  • The train is required for the main desert traversal mission and is optional for most other exploration

Hidden feature: At the final train stop, if you jump onto the roof of the station building before the train departs, you can access a hidden sticker that is otherwise very difficult to reach.

5. Bee Car — Giant House

World: Giant House
Location: In the Giant House kitchen, parked beside the refrigerator
Used in: Kitchen navigation mission, living room exploration

Controls:

  • Left/Right: Steer
  • Forward: Drive forward
  • Jump: Hop (can jump onto countertops)
  • Reverse: Hold back while at low speed

Tips:

  • The Bee Car is sized appropriately for Baaren but is still quite large — some narrow passages require exiting the car
  • The hop jump allows climbing onto kitchen counters, which is required for specific missions
  • Multiple players can ride in the Bee Car in co-op (one front, one back)

Unique mechanic: The Bee Car’s horn (press Interact while driving) triggers certain NPC reactions in the Giant House kitchen, which unlocks a hidden dialogue sequence.

6. Mine Cart — Mole Mines

World: Mole Mines
Location: Available at the mine cart station at the start of Mole Mines
Used in: Main traversal through the mine tunnel sections (required for main path)

Controls:

  • Automatic forward movement
  • Jump: Switch tracks at split points (jump right before the split)
  • Duck: Avoid low-hanging stalactites
  • Lean: Minor speed adjustment on inclines

Tips:

  • Track switches happen fast — recognize the split point well in advance
  • Jumping too early or too late at a split results in staying on the same track
  • Some tracks lead to dead ends (dead-end tracks have extra coins but no exit — you loop back)
  • The highest-gem-yield route in the mine cart takes the right track at split 2 and the left at split 4

Speedrun trick — Cart Exit: Exit the mine cart at split 1 by pressing Jump + Left simultaneously. This lands on a hidden ledge that bypasses the entire cart ride and emerges at the post-cart checkpoint. See the speedrunning guide for exact timing.

Vehicle Comparison

VehicleWorldControl StyleSpeedPassengers
ScooterTurtletownSteerableMedium1
SailboatTurtletown/WaterSteerableVariable2
GliderSnow ValleyPitch/SteerVariable1
Desert TrainBeemothepAutomaticFast1
Bee CarGiant HouseSteerableSlow2
Mine CartMole MinesAutomaticFast1

Vehicles in Multiplayer

In co-op sessions, vehicles work as follows:

  • Scooter, Glider, Mine Cart, Desert Train: Each player has their own instance. Multiple players can use vehicles simultaneously in the same area.
  • Sailboat, Bee Car: Supports 2 players on the same vehicle. Player 1 controls, Player 2 rides.

For co-op setup, see the multiplayer guide.

The Hive Walkthrough: Complete Guide to All Secrets & Honey Mechanics in Super Bear Adventure
Master The Hive world in Super Bear Adventure with our complete walkthrough covering honey mechanics, secret areas, sticker locations, and boss strategies.
How to Complete The Hive in Super Bear Adventure
Complete all missions and master honey mechanics in The Hive
Enter The Hive and learn honey mechanics
Walk into honey to slow down — this is used in puzzles. Honey propulsion pads launch you upward when you stand on them.
Talk to worker bees with quest markers. Collect honey samples and deliver them to the processing area.
Use the elevator and, optionally, the elevator skip (jump onto the upward-moving bee enemy). Complete the honey maze on floor 2.
Find all stickers in The Hive
Check inside honey combs (break them), above the elevator shaft, in the guard bee barracks, in the royal jelly room, and on the throne room ceiling.
Attack the Queen's stinger only during Phase 2 when she enters the hovering state. Ground attacks do minimal damage.

## Introduction to The Hive

The Hive is one of the most visually stunning and mechanically unique worlds in Super Bear Adventure. Set inside a massive beehive structure, this level introduces honey-based gameplay mechanics that set it apart from every other world in the game. From sticky honey walls you can climb to honey slides that launch you across vast gaps, The Hive demands a different approach than worlds like [Turtletown](/blog/turtletown-walkthrough/) or [Snow Valley](/blog/snow-valley-secrets/).

If you are brand new to Super Bear Adventure, we recommend reading our [beginner guide](/blog/how-to-play-beginner-guide/) first to understand the core controls and movement mechanics before tackling The Hive. This world is generally considered mid-to-late game difficulty, so having a solid grasp of the fundamentals will make your experience much smoother.

In this comprehensive walkthrough, we will cover every area of The Hive, explain all honey mechanics in detail, reveal every secret and collectible location, and provide strategies for the world's challenging boss encounter.

## How to Unlock The Hive

![The Hive entrance gate](/images/worlds/detail/hive-entrance.svg)

The Hive becomes accessible after you have completed at least three main missions in the overworld. You can find the entrance on the eastern side of the main hub area, marked by a large honeycomb archway with buzzing bee NPCs nearby. Walk up to the entrance and interact with the gate to enter.

Before heading in, make sure you have collected enough coins to purchase any helpful items from the shop. If you need coins fast, check out our [coin farming guide](/blog/unlimited-coins-farming/) for the most efficient methods. Having a good stock of coins ensures you can buy health refills if the going gets tough inside The Hive.

## Understanding Honey Mechanics

The Hive introduces three distinct honey-based mechanics that you need to master to progress through the world. These mechanics are not found in any other world, making The Hive a truly unique experience.

### Sticky Honey Walls

![Wax wall climbing](/images/worlds/detail/hive-wax-walls.svg)

Throughout The Hive, you will encounter walls coated in a golden, glistening honey substance. When your bear touches these walls, it will stick to them, allowing you to climb vertically and even horizontally across ceilings. The controls while stuck to honey walls are straightforward: use the directional pad or joystick to move in any direction along the surface. Press the jump button to detach from the wall and launch yourself in the direction you are facing.

The key to mastering sticky honey walls is understanding momentum. When you detach from a honey wall, your bear launches with a burst of speed in the direction you were moving. This means you can chain wall jumps by moving upward on one honey wall, detaching, and landing on another honey wall across a gap. Several puzzle sections in The Hive require you to chain three or four wall jumps in sequence to reach high platforms.

### Honey Slides

Honey slides are long, curved channels filled with flowing honey that act as high-speed transport tubes. When you step onto a honey slide, your bear is pulled along the current automatically. You cannot stop once you are on a slide, but you can steer left and right to collect coins and avoid obstacles along the path.

Some honey slides have branching paths. At a fork, you need to lean hard in one direction to take the alternate route. The default path usually leads to the next area of the level, while the branching path often leads to a secret area with bonus collectibles or a hidden sticker. Pay close attention to visual cues at forks -- a faint golden glow on one side often indicates the path to a secret.

### Honey Pools

![Honey pools navigation](/images/worlds/detail/hive-honey-pools.svg)

Honey pools are large bodies of honey that act differently from water in other worlds. Instead of swimming, your bear wades slowly through honey pools. Movement speed is reduced by about 60 percent while in honey, and jumping height is cut in half. This makes honey pools dangerous in areas with enemies, as you cannot dodge or escape quickly.

However, honey pools have a hidden benefit: while standing in honey, your bear gradually regenerates health at a rate of about one heart every five seconds. This can be extremely useful during tough sections where you have taken damage. Just find a safe honey pool without enemies and stand still for a moment to heal up before continuing.

## The Hive Walkthrough: Area by Area

### Area 1: The Entrance Hall

When you first enter The Hive, you find yourself in a large hexagonal chamber with golden walls. The Entrance Hall serves as a tutorial area for honey mechanics. On the left side, you will see your first sticky honey wall with a trail of coins leading upward. Climb this wall to reach the upper platform and collect the first batch of coins.

On the right side, there is a short honey slide that takes you down to a lower section. This slide teaches you the basic steering mechanic. At the bottom, you will find three bee enemies. These are standard worker bees that attack by charging at you. A simple punch combo (three hits) defeats them.

After clearing the enemies, look for a small hexagonal door on the far wall. This is the exit to Area 2. Before leaving, check behind the large honeycomb pillar in the center of the room for a hidden coin cache containing 25 coins.

### Area 2: The Honeycomb Corridors

The Honeycomb Corridors are a maze-like section with multiple paths. The main path is straightforward, but exploring side passages reveals secrets and collectibles. The corridors are populated with worker bees and the more dangerous guard bees, which have a shield and require you to attack from behind.

To navigate efficiently, follow the trail of honey drips on the ceiling. They always point toward the main path forward. When you see a corridor without honey drips, that is a side path worth exploring.

The first major secret in this area is hidden behind a breakable honeycomb wall. Look for a section of wall that has a slightly different shade of gold compared to the surrounding walls. Punch it three times to break through and reveal a small room containing a sticker and a large coin pile. For the complete list of sticker locations, see our [secret stickers guide](/blog/all-secret-stickers-locations/).

### Area 3: The Royal Jelly Chamber

This is the largest area in The Hive and serves as the central hub. The Royal Jelly Chamber is a massive vertical room with platforms spiraling upward around the walls. In the center, a column of golden royal jelly flows from the ceiling to the floor.

The goal here is to reach the top of the chamber. You will need to combine all three honey mechanics to ascend. Start by climbing the sticky honey wall on the left side to reach the first platform. From there, take the honey slide to the opposite wall, where another sticky honey wall awaits. Continue alternating between slides and wall climbs as you spiral upward.

At approximately the halfway point, you will encounter a honey pool platform. This is a good place to heal if you have taken damage from the bee enemies that patrol the platforms. Watch out for the flying guard bees in this section, as they can knock you off platforms and force you to restart the climb.

Near the top of the chamber, there is a tricky section where you must detach from a honey wall and land on a moving platform. The platform follows a circular path and is only in range for about two seconds each cycle. Time your jump carefully, and remember that detaching from a honey wall gives you extra momentum.

### Area 4: The Queen's Antechamber

After reaching the top of the Royal Jelly Chamber, you enter the Queen's Antechamber. This area serves as the pre-boss zone and contains the last collectibles before the boss fight. There are no enemies here, giving you a chance to prepare.

Explore the antechamber thoroughly before proceeding. There are three hidden alcoves in the walls, each containing a small coin cache. One alcove also contains the second-to-last sticker in The Hive. To find the alcoves, look for small cracks in the hexagonal wall pattern and punch them to open passages.

At the far end of the antechamber, you will find a health refill station and a save point. Use both before entering the boss arena through the large ornate door.

### Area 5: The Queen Bee Boss Arena

![Queen Bee boss battle](/images/worlds/detail/hive-boss.svg)

The Queen Bee is the boss of The Hive world. For detailed strategies on this and every other boss in the game, check out our comprehensive [boss fight strategy guide](/blog/boss-fight-strategy/). Here, we will cover the essentials specific to this encounter.

The Queen Bee fight takes place in a circular arena with honey walls around the perimeter and a honey pool in the center. The fight has three phases, each introducing new attack patterns.

In phase one, the Queen Bee flies around the arena and periodically dive-bombs your position. Dodge to the side when you see her wind up (she pauses in the air for about one second before diving). After a dive, she is stunned for about three seconds, giving you a window to attack. Three hits during each stun window advances her to the next phase.

In phase two, the Queen Bee summons swarms of worker bees while continuing her dive attacks. The worker bees come in waves of three. Focus on dodging the Queen's dives and deal with the workers between dive attacks. The Queen still gets stunned after dives, but the stun window is shorter at about two seconds.

In phase three, the Queen Bee adds a honey spray attack. She hovers in the center of the arena and sprays honey in a rotating pattern. The honey creates temporary sticky patches on the ground that slow your movement. Use the honey walls around the arena to climb above the spray and then drop down on the Queen when she finishes spraying. This is your attack window in phase three, and three hits during this window will defeat her.

## All Secrets in The Hive

The Hive contains a total of 12 secrets spread across its five areas. Finding all of them contributes to your overall completion percentage and unlocks a special cosmetic reward. For a broader look at secrets across all worlds, visit our [secret areas and easter eggs guide](/blog/secret-areas-easter-eggs/).

### Secret 1: Hidden Coin Room (Entrance Hall)

Behind the central pillar in the Entrance Hall, punch the back wall to reveal a hidden room with 50 coins.

### Secret 2: Breakable Wall Sticker (Honeycomb Corridors)

In the Honeycomb Corridors, find the differently-shaded wall section and break through to find a sticker and coins.

### Secret 3: Ceiling Passage (Honeycomb Corridors)

In the third corridor from the entrance, there is a sticky honey patch on the ceiling. Climb across it to reach a hidden upper passage with a large gem worth 100 coins.

### Secret 4: Reverse Honey Slide (Honeycomb Corridors)

One of the honey slides in the corridors can be entered from the exit end by performing a well-timed jump. Going backward through the slide reveals a hidden alcove midway through with a rare collectible.

### Secret 5: False Floor (Royal Jelly Chamber)

On the third platform while spiraling upward, one of the hexagonal floor tiles is slightly raised. Step on it to trigger a trapdoor that drops you into a secret room below with a sticker and 75 coins. Use the honey wall inside the room to climb back up.

### Secret 6: Behind the Royal Jelly Column

When you reach the midpoint of the Royal Jelly Chamber, jump into the royal jelly column itself. Instead of taking damage, you are transported to a hidden room behind the column containing a rare hat unlock. Check our [skins and hats guide](/blog/unlock-all-skins-hats/) for the full list of unlockable cosmetics.

### Secret 7: Moving Platform Secret (Royal Jelly Chamber)

Instead of jumping onto the moving platform near the top of the chamber, wait for it to pass and then jump to the small ledge behind where the platform docks. A hidden passage leads to a bonus area with 150 coins.

### Secret 8: Double-Back Corridor (Royal Jelly Chamber)

After reaching the top of the chamber, instead of going forward into the antechamber, look behind you for a small honey wall leading to a separate upper area with a sticker.

### Secret 9: Alcove Sticker (Queen's Antechamber)

One of the three wall alcoves in the antechamber contains a sticker. Check the right wall first.

### Secret 10: Floor Pattern Puzzle (Queen's Antechamber)

Step on the hexagonal floor tiles in a specific pattern (center, top-right, bottom-left, top-left, bottom-right, center) to open a hidden compartment in the floor containing a golden honeycomb collectible worth 200 coins.

### Secret 11: Boss Arena Honey Pool (Boss Arena)

During the boss fight, if you stand in the center honey pool for ten consecutive seconds without being hit, a secret platform rises from the pool with a sticker on it.

### Secret 12: Post-Boss Secret Room

After defeating the Queen Bee, instead of going through the victory door immediately, return to the entrance of the arena. A previously blocked passage is now open, leading to a room with the final sticker and a large coin reward.

## Tips for Completing The Hive

Mastering The Hive comes down to comfort with the honey mechanics. Here are some additional tips to help you through.

### Movement Tips

Always keep moving on honey walls. Staying still causes your bear to slowly slide downward due to gravity. Maintain upward momentum by constantly pressing in the direction you want to go.

On honey slides, position yourself toward the outer edge of curves to maintain maximum speed. The inner edge slows you down slightly, which can cause you to miss jump opportunities at the end of slides.

### Combat Tips

Guard bees are best handled by using the honey walls to get above them and then dropping down with a ground pound attack. This bypasses their front shield entirely.

During the Queen Bee fight, stay near the arena walls so you can quickly climb to safety during the honey spray attack in phase three.

### Collection Tips

Do not worry about getting every secret on your first playthrough. You can replay The Hive at any time from the world select menu to pick up anything you missed. Focus on completing the main path first and then return for collectibles.

If you are struggling with the platforming sections, the mod APK version available on our [homepage](/) provides additional tools and abilities that can make these sections more manageable. Features like enhanced jump height or slow-motion can help you nail tricky wall jump sequences.

## How The Hive Connects to the Larger Game

The Hive is more than just a standalone world. Completing it unlocks several things in the broader Super Bear Adventure experience. The Queen Bee's crown, obtained after defeating the boss, is required for one of the late-game missions in the overworld. This mission is covered in our [all missions guide](/blog/all-missions-guide/).

Additionally, The Hive's visual style and honey mechanics make callbacks in the [Arcade World](/blog/arcade-world-mini-games/), where one of the mini-games is a honey-slide racing challenge. Performing well in this mini-game is easier if you have already mastered the honey slide mechanics in The Hive itself.

The lore of The Hive also ties into the broader story of Baaren and the bear kingdom. The Queen Bee is one of the key characters in the game's narrative, and her dialogue after the boss fight reveals important plot details. If you are interested in the full story, our [lore and story guide](/blog/lore-baaren-story/) covers everything in detail.

## Conclusion

The Hive is a standout world in Super Bear Adventure that rewards players who take the time to master its unique honey mechanics. With sticky walls, fast-paced slides, and strategic honey pools, this world offers a gameplay experience unlike anything else in the game. By following this walkthrough, you should be able to navigate every area, find every secret, and defeat the Queen Bee without too much trouble.

If you are ready to continue your adventure, the [Mole Mines](/blog/mole-mines-walkthrough/) is a great next stop with its own set of unique mechanics and challenges. And if you are experiencing any performance issues while playing, our [lag fix and optimization guide](/blog/lag-fix-optimization/) can help you get the smoothest possible experience on any device.

After defeating the Queen Bee, instead of going through the victory door immediately, return to the entrance of the arena. A previously blocked passage is now open, leading to a room with the final sticker and a large coin reward.

Tips for Completing The Hive

Mastering The Hive comes down to comfort with the honey mechanics. Here are some additional tips to help you through.

Movement Tips

Always keep moving on honey walls. Staying still causes your bear to slowly slide downward due to gravity. Maintain upward momentum by constantly pressing in the direction you want to go.

On honey slides, position yourself toward the outer edge of curves to maintain maximum speed. The inner edge slows you down slightly, which can cause you to miss jump opportunities at the end of slides.

Combat Tips

Guard bees are best handled by using the honey walls to get above them and then dropping down with a ground pound attack. This bypasses their front shield entirely.

During the Queen Bee fight, stay near the arena walls so you can quickly climb to safety during the honey spray attack in phase three.

Collection Tips

Do not worry about getting every secret on your first playthrough. You can replay The Hive at any time from the world select menu to pick up anything you missed. Focus on completing the main path first and then return for collectibles.

If you are struggling with the platforming sections, the mod APK version available on our homepage provides additional tools and abilities that can make these sections more manageable. Features like enhanced jump height or slow-motion can help you nail tricky wall jump sequences.

How The Hive Connects to the Larger Game

The Hive is more than just a standalone world. Completing it unlocks several things in the broader Super Bear Adventure experience. The Queen Bee’s crown, obtained after defeating the boss, is required for one of the late-game missions in the overworld. This mission is covered in our all missions guide.

Additionally, The Hive’s visual style and honey mechanics make callbacks in the Arcade World, where one of the mini-games is a honey-slide racing challenge. Performing well in this mini-game is easier if you have already mastered the honey slide mechanics in The Hive itself.

The lore of The Hive also ties into the broader story of Baaren and the bear kingdom. The Queen Bee is one of the key characters in the game’s narrative, and her dialogue after the boss fight reveals important plot details. If you are interested in the full story, our lore and story guide covers everything in detail.

Conclusion

The Hive is a standout world in Super Bear Adventure that rewards players who take the time to master its unique honey mechanics. With sticky walls, fast-paced slides, and strategic honey pools, this world offers a gameplay experience unlike anything else in the game. By following this walkthrough, you should be able to navigate every area, find every secret, and defeat the Queen Bee without too much trouble.

If you are ready to continue your adventure, the Mole Mines is a great next stop with its own set of unique mechanics and challenges. And if you are experiencing any performance issues while playing, our lag fix and optimization guide can help you get the smoothest possible experience on any device.
